tasks.context:
Observed 2026-04-08 during session 6ffd73be: user reported ruflo MCP failing to reconnect. /mcp dialog dismissed (either ruflo not offered or reconnect did not stick).

Recurrence of conditions from t-961 (completed 2026-04-06). t-961 mitigation: system/scripts/ruflo-mcp.sh auto-restarts on SIGTERM up to MAX_RESTARTS=5. Hypothesis: restart cap hit, or a new failure mode bypasses the wrapper restart loop.

Initial session probes:
- No ruflo/claude-flow processes running (pgrep -fa ruflo empty)
- ~/.swarm/memory.db present, last written 11:25 today (so ruflo was alive earlier in session)
- .mcp.json correctly points to system/scripts/ruflo-mcp.sh
- Skill search returned 0.1 top score — no existing skill for this diagnosis flow

Key files:
- system/scripts/ruflo-mcp.sh (wrapper with auto-restart)
- .mcp.json
- ~/.swarm/memory.db
- ~/.swarm/ruflo-mcp.pid (advisory lock)

Refs: t-961, feedback_cc-sigterm-mcp-bug.md, anthropics/claude-code#40207, docs/ideas/fast-cold-start.md

Next: diagnose whether wrapper hit restart cap, whether ruflo binary itself errors, or whether CC dropped the server and /mcp did not re-register it.

---
Diagnosis 2026-04-08 (session 6ffd73be):

Layer-by-layer check:
- Ruflo binary v3.5.48 at /home/martineserios/.nvm/versions/node/v20.19.0/bin/ruflo — healthy, --version works
- Wrapper system/scripts/ruflo-mcp.sh — launches ruflo cleanly: "[mcp-...] Starting in stdio mode"
- ~/.swarm/memory.db — intact, last write 11:25 today (ruflo was alive earlier in session)
- ~/.swarm/ruflo-mcp.pid — absent (clean, no stale lock)
- Ruflo processes — none running (pgrep -fa ruflo empty)
- CC session MCP tool registry — ruflo absent. Deferred tools list shows mcp__brana__* and mcp__google-sheets__* but NO mcp__ruflo__* tools.

Root cause: Ruflo process was killed mid-session (likely CC SIGTERM #40207). This time CC did not re-register the server on /mcp — from CC's perspective ruflo is simply gone. The t-961 wrapper mitigation (auto-restart up to 5x) only helps if CC keeps the stdio pipe open. Once CC drops a server from its MCP list, wrapper-side restarts cannot recover it — CC must re-initialize the connection itself.

Why /mcp dialog did not help: The dialog likely enumerated only servers CC still tracked. If ruflo was already dropped from CC's internal registry (not merely marked unhealthy), there is nothing to reconnect via the dialog.

Recovery path tried this session: none — all probes were read-only. Recovery requires CC session restart OR a /mcp reconnect if ruflo is still listed.

Follow-up questions for investigation (not answered this session):
1. Does CC expose any API/event to detect "server dropped from registry" vs "server unhealthy"? If yes, we could surface a clearer signal.
2. Can the wrapper preemptively write a "CC must restart" marker somewhere the statusline can read?
3. Is there a way to get CC to re-initialize a specific MCP server without full session restart? (Upstream CC feature request.)


---
Fix 2026-04-08 (session 15ef229f):

Original diagnosis was wrong. Ruflo wasn't dropped mid-session — the wrapper had been broken since 2026-04-06 17:45 (t-961/t-982/t-988 commits). Every session since then has started with a non-functional ruflo. The `memory.db` 11:25 mtime was from bulk-index scripts, not a live MCP connection.

Real root cause: system/scripts/ruflo-mcp.sh used `"$RUFLO" "$@" &` + `wait $PID` to support SIGTERM/SIGHUP restart loops. Backgrounding in a non-interactive bash script reading from a pipe silently breaks stdin delivery to the child — ruflo logs "Starting in stdio mode" then never receives the JSON-RPC initialize request. CC times out the handshake and marks the server failed.

Reproduction (confirmed):
- `(printf JSON; sleep 4) | bash ruflo-mcp.sh mcp start` → only log line, no init response
- Same input piped to minimal `exec "$RUFLO" "$@"` wrapper → proper initialize reply
- Isolated: backgrounding alone (no traps) also breaks it — not a trap/signal issue

Fix applied: replaced the entire restart loop with `exec "$RUFLO" "$@"`. Dropped SIGTERM/SIGHUP auto-restart — that logic never actually recovered from CC #40207 anyway (existing memory note already says `/mcp` reconnect is the real recovery path), and it's pure downside when it breaks the handshake permanently.

Kept: CWD fix (cd $HOME before exec — critical for AgentDB path), nvm resolution, advisory PID file.

Verified post-fix: wrapper now returns `{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{...}}` on stdin init. Full handshake completes.

Files changed:
- system/scripts/ruflo-mcp.sh (76 lines → 28 lines)

Recovery for current session: restart CC — this session's ruflo is still dead, the fix only takes effect on next session start.

Follow-ups:
- Add a shell test under tests/hooks/ that pipes a JSON-RPC init to the wrapper and asserts a response comes back
- Revisit SIGTERM/SIGHUP restart as a separate feature if CC #40207 recurs (probably unnecessary)
- Update memory note feedback_cc-sigterm-mcp-bug.md to note the wrapper no longer attempts auto-restart

---
Resolution 2026-04-08 (during same session, parallel work):

Root cause corrected: wrapper was backgrounding ruflo (`ruflo & wait`) to support SIGTERM/SIGHUP restart loop from t-961. Backgrounding broke stdin forwarding — ruflo never received CC's MCP handshake reliably. My earlier "CC registry drop" diagnosis was a downstream symptom.

Fix deployed: system/scripts/ruflo-mcp.sh now uses `exec "$RUFLO" "$@"` (line 28). Auto-restart loop removed. Stdin/stdout pipes are preserved via exec, so CC's handshake reaches ruflo directly.

Supersedes: the t-961 MAX_RESTARTS=5 wrapper mitigation. Restart loop is gone.

New patterns written (timestamp 17:25):
- feedback_mcp-stdio-exec-pattern.md — MCP stdio wrappers must use exec, not background+wait
- feedback_preflight-warnings-over-midskill-crash.md — surface deterministic env failures at session-start

MEMORY.md updated:
- CC SIGTERM bug entry now notes "Restart loop removed (see t-1083)"
- New bullet: "MCP stdio wrappers must use exec, not background+wait"

Verification pending: this CC session is still running the old wrapper instance (launched at session start). Restart CC to pick up the new wrapper. On next session start, confirm mcp__ruflo__* tools reappear in deferred tools list.
tasks.notes: Fix deployed in same session via parallel work: system/scripts/ruflo-mcp.sh uses exec pattern (line 28), restart loop removed, supersedes t-961 MAX_RESTARTS mitigation. Verification requires CC restart to pick up new wrapper. See feedback_mcp-stdio-exec-pattern.md.

tasks.description: Conditional fallback for the no-attribution enforcement stack. The current stack (deployed 2026-04-09) has 4 active layers: (1) MEMORY.md preference, (2) system/rules/git-discipline.md HARD RULE, (3) CC settings.json attribution.commit/.pr='', (4) brana PreToolUse hook on Bash 'git commit'. Layer 5 (git pre-commit + commit-msg hooks at ~/.config/git/hooks/) is DEPLOYED but NOT activated globally. User explicitly rejected 'git config --global core.hooksPath ~/.config/git/hooks' because: (a) not portable — global git config is per-machine, can't be version-controlled, doesn't follow the user to new dev environments (laptop, server, fresh install), (b) global side effects on every repo on the machine including non-brana ones, (c) potential conflict with existing per-repo hooks. RE-EVALUATE WHEN: a co-author / Co-Authored-By / Signed-off-by / 'Generated with' trailer slips through despite Layers 1-4 active. If that happens, the path forward is NOT global core.hooksPath. Instead, do one of: (i) extend bootstrap.sh to install pre-commit + commit-msg into each brana-related repo's .git/hooks/ during bootstrap (per-repo deployment, portable, scriptable, scoped to brana repos only) — this requires enumerating the set of 'brana-related repos' which is currently thebrana + brana-knowledge + each client + each venture; (ii) write a small 'brana repo init' helper that any new repo runs to install hooks; (iii) investigate a brana-side intercept that can't be bypassed (e.g., wrapping git itself via PATH shim, but that has its own portability issues). KEEP DEFERRED: do nothing until evidence of failure. Files: ~/.config/git/hooks/pre-commit (exists, dormant), system/scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit (source), bootstrap.sh Step 4d (deployment logic). User preference noted 2026-04-09: portability > convenience for enforcement layers.
tasks.notes: Decision (Wave 2 pre-flight): KEEP DEFERRED. Audit of all commits since 2026-04-09 (Layers 1-4 deployment date) shows zero co-author / signed-off / generated-with trailer leakage in actual commit messages (the only matches are inside the rule-doc commit 84726e4 itself, listing forbidden patterns). Layers 1-4 are holding. No need to escalate to per-repo hook deployment (Layer 5) at this time. Re-evaluate trigger remains: any single trailer slip in production commits will surface the choice between bootstrap.sh per-repo install vs brana-side intercept. Source: a6145c3 (Apr 6) was pre-stack; that's not counted.
tasks.subject: Decide: per-repo git hook deployment if Layers 1-4 still let co-author trailers slip

tasks.description: Build a friction classifier over brana session history. Cluster E (URL batch research 2026-04-08) called this "highest-leverage addition" not currently in brana. Source: Rudel.ai analytics finding — 26% of CC sessions abandoned in first 60s; Paul Duvall 291 sessions → ~1 commit/session as leading productivity indicator. Raw material already exists: brana session JSONL + session_history MCP tool. Three sub-decisions: SD1=what to classify (metrics-only counts vs LLM outcome classification vs rich friction points), SD2=where to surface (inside /brana:review vs standalone /brana:insights vs both), SD3=action trigger (surface data only vs suggest workflow changes). Shape docs: docs/research/2026-04-08-url-batch-findings.md §Cluster E. Safe start: Option C metrics-only (no LLM cost, pure counts from existing JSONL). Add outcome classification (Option A) later once data collection is validated. Aligns with "knowledge retrieval tax" framing — sessions that waste time re-orienting are tax. If brana can identify which patterns correlate with wasted re-orientation, that is directly actionable.
tasks.subject: Session analytics / /brana:insights — friction classifier on session history

tasks.description: Brana has no resume story for long-running skill procedures. A /brana:build crash at phase 5 of 6 means restarting from phase 1. Source: Kitaru project (Cluster C, URL batch research 2026-04-08) — @Checkpoint/@Flow decorators write a minimal JSONL step log at phase boundaries. Each step has an idempotent ID. On resume, runner reads the log, skips completed steps, picks up where it left off. Brana equivalent: write ~/.claude/run-state/{task_id}.jsonl, one line per completed step with timestamp + step ID. On skill start, check if run-state file exists for active task — if yes, fast-forward past completed steps. On clean skill end, delete the file. Key dependency: /brana:build procedure needs explicit step markers (not a huge change). Secondary value: makes long-running skills observable mid-run, not just at end. Pairs naturally with auto-learning loop — EXTRACT can run at each checkpoint boundary, not just session close. Shape docs: docs/research/2026-04-08-url-batch-findings.md §Cluster C.
tasks.subject: Checkpoint/resume for skill procedures — no recovery story for long /brana:build runs

tasks.description: Brana has ~35 skills and zero usage data. CC leak confirmed Anthropic's own initBundledSkills() is tight — they ship a small focused set. Every skill adds tokens to system prompt. Skills with zero 30-day invocations are pure overhead. Source: skill-kit (Cluster D, URL batch 2026-04-08) — local CLI reading CC JSONL logs for skill usage stats. Brana equivalent: `brana skills usage` CLI subcommand — scan ~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl, count skill invocations by name over rolling 30 days, output ranked list with last-used date. Secondary value: routing failures visible — if /brana:do is supposed to route freeform input but users invoke skills directly 90% of the time, the router is not working. ADR-035 decision: what invocation threshold triggers a cull recommendation? Suggested: <5 invocations in 30 days = cull candidate. Implementation: same JSONL scanner pattern as `brana session history` (Rust). Low technical risk, high clarity payoff. Shape docs: docs/research/2026-04-08-url-batch-findings.md §Cluster D, docs/research/2026-04-08-cc-alignment-findings.md §D6.
tasks.subject: Skill usage telemetry — ADR-035 candidate, evidence-based pruning

tasks.description: Vasilev argument (Cluster D, URL batch 2026-04-08): most MCPs are help-surface layers for tools that already have CLIs. Measured GitHub MCP at ~54K tokens vs gh --help at ~562. Help-surface MCP consumes context budget agent needs for reasoning. CC blocks on ALL MCP servers at startup (confirmed in brana MEMORY.md) — every MCP in .mcp.json adds blocking startup time. Brana currently loads 5 MCPs always. ADR-036 decision rule: if interaction is stateless help/command → CLI. If stateful session/connection/live index → MCP. Audit of brana's current stack: brana-mcp=KEEP (structured JSON, 65% fewer tokens, battle-tested), ruflo=KEEP (stateful, connection-based, load-bearing), linkedin-mcp=MIGRATE (brittle, rate limits, patchright auth, 24h blocks — prime CLI candidate), context7=AUDIT (help-surface layer for library docs?), google-sheets=AUDIT (could be brana gsheets CLI + keyring). This task = write the decision rule as ADR-036 + run the audit + produce migration plan for linkedin-mcp. Goal: reduce cold-start blocking and context overhead. Shape docs: docs/research/2026-04-08-url-batch-findings.md §Cluster D.
tasks.notes: ADR-036 written 2026-04-10. Decision rule: stateless → CLI, stateful → MCP. Audit: brana-mcp + ruflo KEEP. google-sheets needs ADR-033 fix (uvx → pinned binary). linkedin-mcp + context7 explicitly retired (already absent from configs). No CLI migration of google-sheets — fix the pin, not the shape.
tasks.subject: MCP-vs-CLI audit — ADR-036 candidate, linkedin-mcp prime migration target

tasks.description: Best positioning frame from the 2026-04-08 URL batch research. Source: Francisco Marques da Silva (coined), amplified by Lindenberg (Cluster B). The argument: every CC session, the LLM re-infers domain constraints it already knows — tech stack, naming conventions, past decisions. This re-inference work is the "knowledge retrieval tax" — invisible, constant, compounding. Brana's CLAUDE.md + dimensions + ruflo search IS the answer to this tax. But brana has never named it that way. Current positioning ("harness with opinions", "cross-client memory") is accurate but abstract. "Eliminate the knowledge retrieval tax" is concrete and immediately legible to any developer who has experienced the "explain yourself again" problem. Why this is load-bearing beyond marketing: the frame clarifies what brana optimizes for. Every design decision becomes "does this reduce the retrieval tax or not?" LOAD step → reduces tax. Lint+Heal → reduces tax (contradictions + orphans increase it). Session memory alignment → reduces tax. The frame is a design compass, not just a headline. Task = draft a positioning doc + 2-3 LinkedIn post angles. Tag Lindenberg + Marques da Silva on publication. Creators: linkedin.com/in/alindnbrg (Lindenberg), linkedin.com/in/franciscomarquessilva (Marques da Silva). Shape docs: docs/research/2026-04-08-url-batch-findings.md §Cluster B.
tasks.subject: Knowledge retrieval tax — positioning frame + content angle

tasks.description: Aum post "12 Agentic Harness Patterns" Pattern 10: Command Risk Classification — safe auto-run / risky approval / dangerous blocked. CC handles this via its 4-tier permission cascade (static rules → tool logic → permission mode → classifier). Brana currently only uses tier 1 (static rules in settings.json). The opportunity: a PreToolUse hook that detects risky patterns BEFORE CC's permission system has to — a complementary layer, not a replacement. Examples: Bash commands that write to /etc, git push --force, rm -rf, database drops. These could be flagged by brana's hook with a richer context message than CC's generic "approve?" prompt. Source: docs/research/2026-04-08-cc-alignment-findings.md §addendum D9. Parked — low priority, no acute pain. Worth building after D4 (PreQuery/PostQuery) is resolved on a future CC version, since per-turn hooks are the right granularity for this.
tasks.subject: Command risk classifier hook — Aum Pattern 10, brana complement to CC permission tiers

tasks.description: Karpathy's pipeline step 1→3: Sources → raw/ → WIKI. Brana equivalent: event log + inbox/ → brana-knowledge/dimensions/. Currently the pipeline is manual and stops short — /brana:research fetches and synthesizes but doesn't write the final dimension doc. URLs, emails, audio transcripts, and session learnings all land in the event log without a typed model. The chosen methodology (user decision 2026-04-08): LLM drafts and maintains dimension docs (Layer 2). Humans steer, not write. ADRs and rules (Layer 1) stay human-authored. This task: design and implement the pipeline. Key sub-decisions: (1) staging location for drafts — brana-knowledge/drafts/ vs per-project, (2) draft frontmatter convention — what status:draft means and what metadata is required, (3) promotion ritual — how a draft moves to accepted (CLI command? skill step? hook?), (4) review cadence — /brana:review weekly or on-demand. DEPENDENCY: shares draft staging directory design with D2 (t-1075 Lint+Heal). Both must agree on staging location and promotion ritual before either is implemented at L3+. Shape doc: docs/ideas/inbox-to-dimensions-pipeline.md (exists). Source: docs/research/2026-04-08-cc-alignment-findings.md §second addendum D10.
tasks.subject: inbox→dimensions pipeline — Karpathy sources→wiki, LLM drafts Layer 2 docs from raw inputs

tasks.description: Statusbar shows two conflicting context signals: "⚠ CTX 78%" and "7% until auto-compact" in the bottom line. These should agree — if context is 78% used, remaining is 22%, not 7%. One of the two calculations is wrong or they're reading different metrics. Repro: observed on session 2026-04-10, branch docs/t-1105-ecosystem-map.
tasks.subject: Fix statusbar context% misalignment — CTX% and auto-compact% show different values

tasks.description: Greenfield script. Implements L2 deterministic Lint+Heal. Capabilities: (1) dedup — exact name: frontmatter match WITHIN same git root only; cross-path-variant dirs excluded; archive to ~/.claude/memory/archive/YYYY-MM-DD/; (2) grep contradiction detection — feedback_*.md + project_*.md only, MEMORY.md excluded; (3) frontmatter imputation — fill missing name:/description:/type: via heuristics; (4) concept-reference surfacing — phrase ≥10 occurrences across MEMORY.md files, no dedicated doc → add to report. Non-negotiables: --dry-run flag (always safe from terminal); lock file ~/.swarm/lint-heal.lock (PID + stale detection); rollback snapshot before writes; gate state ~/.swarm/lint-heal-state.json {last_run_ts, session_count_since_run}; Layer 2 only (hardcoded allow-list of writable paths).
tasks.notes: Stale projects/ dirs confirmed (2026-04-10): projects/nexeye, projects/proyecto-anita, projects/proyecto_anita are old paths — moved to clients/. Dedup rule: when same name: appears under projects/ AND clients/, archive the projects/ copy. Tiebreak by mtime — archive less-recently-modified file.
tasks.subject: Write lint-heal.sh — dedup + grep contradiction + frontmatter imputation + concept surfacing

Lock file required. Depends on t-1113-a (knowledge_pipeline.rs). Prompt spec in feature brief. Blocked by: brana-core subtask.

tasks.description: After each CC Task step completes, the command halts and waits for user to type "continue" before proceeding to the next step. Expected behavior: steps should chain automatically unless a step requires user input (e.g. backlog review, challenger approval). Root cause likely in how the step registry is structured — each step probably ends with a TaskUpdate and then the model yields control back to the user instead of proceeding. Fix: ensure multi-step skills auto-advance through non-interactive steps without pausing. Interactive steps (backlog review, store findings, challenger) should still pause for input.

tasks.context:
Branch: claude/newsletter-email-subscription-0mtXU (clean, nothing committed yet).

Goal: newsletters subscribed via Gmail flow into brana as log entries + searchable body.

## What already exists (do NOT reinvent)
- brana inbox: Gmail IMAP, headers-only, writes inbox-log.jsonl. No action routing, no body.
- brana feed: RSS/Atom, has --action log|task per-feed. log→feed-log.jsonl, task→brana backlog add.
- brana log / /brana:log: event-log.md, auto-detects URLs, archives >90d.
- Scheduler: inbox-poll job exists at 09:00, disabled by default.

## The gap
brana inbox poll writes to a siloed JSONL, headers only. Does NOT feed event-log.md, does NOT extract body, does NOT route per-subscription, does NOT surface in /brana:sitrep.

## Design — resolved decisions

D (routing) is the keystone — mirror feed --action exactly:
- Per-subscription: --action log|task|archive|skip
- skip/archive: stay header-only (no body fetch)
- log|task: fetch plaintext body (HTML stripped)
- Default action: skip (gracefully handles non-newsletter emails)

A (where entries live): log action → dual-write
- Headline in event-log.md (keeps it scannable)
- Full body in ~/.claude/memory/newsletters/YYYY-MM.md
- + ruflo indexing for semantic retrieval (v2)

B (how much body): plaintext-stripped HTML, no truncation for stored body. LLM summary is v2.

C (scope): newsletter-first. General inbox (receipts, alerts) handled via skip default — no redesign needed to add later.

## Open questions (not yet resolved)
E: Unread state — "N unread newsletters" in /brana:sitrep? Likely: count log-routed messages since last sitrep read.
F: Subscription model — manual add v1 (same as feed), auto-discover from Gmail Newsletters label v2.

## MVP scope
1. Extend brana inbox poll to fetch plaintext body (HTML stripped) when action=log|task
2. Add --action log|task|archive|skip to brana inbox add (default: skip)
3. action=log → headline in event-log.md + full body in newsletters/YYYY-MM.md
4. action=task → brana backlog add with body as context, tags=[newsletter, sender-slug]
5. Enable inbox-poll scheduler job once an account is configured
6. Surface N unread newsletters in /brana:sitrep

Skipped for v1: ruflo indexing, LLM summaries, auto-discover, HTML rendering.

## Key files
- system/cli/rust/crates/brana-cli/src/commands/inbox.rs (extend poll + add)
- system/cli/rust/crates/brana-cli/src/commands/feed.rs (mirror action pattern)
- system/procedures/log.md (dual-write target for log action)
- docs/architecture/features/scheduler.md (enable inbox-poll job)
- ~/.claude/scheduler/inbox.json (per-account config)
tasks.notes: Brainstormed 2026-04-13. Design order: D→A→B→C→E→F. D (routing) is keystone — once per-subscription action exists, everything else follows. Mirrors feed.rs pattern exactly.
tasks.subject: inbox-v2 — full triage CLI (read, archive, mark-read, search, open)

tasks.context:
CONCERN: brana system may be writing to CC files in ways that violate their intended purpose.

Claude Code file purposes (reference):
- ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md — global identity/principles, applies to ALL projects. Should contain: user preferences, cross-client rules, portfolio index, agent roster. Should NOT contain: project-specific content, field notes, session artifacts.
- {project}/.claude/CLAUDE.md — project instructions + conventions. Checked into git, travels with repo. Should contain: architecture decisions, commands, field notes that REQUIRE this repo context. Should NOT contain: transferable rules (those go to ~/.claude/rules/), operational logs.
- ~/.claude/projects/{slug}/memory/MEMORY.md — cross-session pattern index. Should contain: behavioral rules, pointers to pattern files, architectural facts. Should NOT contain: derivable info, git history, task lists, code patterns.
- ~/.claude/projects/{slug}/memory/feedback_*.md — individual pattern files (problem/solution/confidence). Written by /brana:close Step 5.
- ~/.claude/rules/*.md — behavioral rules loaded at session start for ALL projects. Should contain: "always X", "never Y" directives only.
- ~/.claude/settings.json — CC config (hooks, tools, MCP). Not a knowledge store.

SPECIFIC CONCERN (raised 2026-04-13): Field notes being written to .claude/CLAUDE.md. The heuristic (useful without this repo? → dimension doc, else → CLAUDE.md) may still produce wrong routing — e.g. operational gotchas that are actually transferable rules might land in CLAUDE.md instead of rules/ or feedback_*.md.

SCOPE OF AUDIT:
1. Read CC docs on each file type purpose (claude.ai/docs or local CC help)
2. Inventory what brana currently writes to each file (close.md, reconcile.md, bootstrap.sh)
3. For each write site: does the content match the file purpose?
4. Identify mismatches — content in wrong file, or routing heuristic producing wrong classifications
5. Fix routing logic in close.md Step 5/5b/6 and any other write sites
6. Update field notes in MEMORY.md or CLAUDE.md if they should be in rules/ instead

REF: Session discussion 2026-04-13, /brana:close field notes routing.
tasks.subject: Audit brana writes against Claude Code file purposes

tasks.context: Currently brana knowledge process --tier1 processes one batch of 50 URLs serially via Claude CLI. With large intake batches (40+ URLs as of 2026-04-13), parallel execution would reduce total tier1 time significantly. Approach: split unprocessed URLs into concurrent batches, spawn multiple claude -p calls in parallel (std::thread::scope pattern already used in brana-cli). Key files: system/cli/rust/crates/brana-core/src/knowledge_pipeline.rs (tier1 batch logic), system/cli/rust/crates/brana-cli/src/commands/knowledge.rs (CLI entrypoint). Constraint: respect pipeline lock to avoid state corruption.

tasks.description:
Official Anthropic recommendation: use ConfigChange hook type to audit/block settings changes during sessions. Prevents in-session permission escalation.

Not currently in system/hooks/hooks.json. Needs SDD spec before implementation (behavioral change).

Context:
- hooks.json location: system/hooks/hooks.json
- Hook type: "ConfigChange" (new event type, different from PreToolUse/PostToolUse)
- Behavior: log or block settings changes (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL changes are the key risk vector)
- Source: official security docs + research session 2026-04-14, Scout E + Deep-2

Security motivation: CVE-2026-21852 exploits ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL manipulation. ConfigChange hook would catch this at the source.

tasks.description:
Official security docs: write scope is CWD-anchored but read is unrestricted. Starting CC in ~/enter_thebrana/ expands write blast radius to entire portfolio.

Rule content: "Always start Claude Code from the project root directory (e.g., ~/enter_thebrana/thebrana/), never from a parent workspace directory. The write permission blast radius equals your CWD + all subfolders."

File: system/rules/cwd-discipline.md
Requires worktree pattern (rules/ is behavioral, intercepted by main-guard.sh).

Source: official security docs, research session 2026-04-14 Deep-2
tasks.subject: Add CWD discipline rule to system/rules/

tasks.description:
CVE chain: malicious hook → sets env var → attacker MCP server exfiltrates secrets. ADR-033 pins MCP via wrapper scripts, but validate.sh doesn't enforce this.

Add to validate.sh:
- Parse .mcp.json
- Flag any entry using npx, uvx, or non-pinned paths
- Fail with message: "Non-pinned MCP entry detected: [entry]. Use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/*.sh wrapper per ADR-033."

This closes the gap between the ADR (which says to pin) and enforcement (which currently relies on convention only).

Source: ADR-033, Check Point Research, research session 2026-04-14

tasks.context:
t-1238 session (2026-04-14) produced a replicable planning methodology that should be encoded into the standard brana workflow:

The pattern:
1. INVESTIGATE — /brana:backlog start → freeform design session, produce taxonomy/design
2. CHALLENGE (round 1) — /brana:challenge on the design → surfaces critical flaws, challenger agent
3. RESHAPE — address all findings, produce revised plan
4. CHALLENGE (round 2) — /brana:challenge on the reshaped plan → pre-mortem + simplicity pass
5. ADDRESS — incorporate all findings and warnings
6. PLAN — /brana:backlog plan with full hierarchy (waves → milestones → tasks)
7. LIFECYCLE — DDD + SDD + tests + docs wired as tasks before every implementation task
8. INTEGRATE — include updates to all commands/workflows affected by the change (Phase D pattern)

What to update:
- /brana:backlog plan procedure: add challenge gate before task creation for M+ efforts (currently no challenge step). Gate: if proposed phase has M+ effort tasks, require /brana:challenge before WRITE step.
- /brana:build procedure: enforce DDD→SDD→tests exist in backlog before CLASSIFY step on M+ tasks.
- /brana:backlog start procedure: for investigation strategy, suggest double-challenge pattern explicitly.
- ARCHITECTURE.md: add field note documenting this as the validated planning methodology.
- MEMORY.md: add pointer to this pattern.

Evidence: t-1238 collapsed 8→5 tasks via double challenger, added 8 lifecycle tasks (DDD/SDD/tests/docs) that were missing from initial plan, added Phase D (workflow integration) that was missing entirely. Without the methodology, the plan would have shipped with ruflo as a load-bearing dependency (known-unreliable), no tests, and no workflow integration.
tasks.subject: Encode t-1238 planning methodology — update /brana:backlog plan + /brana:build

tasks.description:
Several skills only make sense when CWD is the thebrana repo: /brana:maintain-specs, /brana:apply-errata, /brana:re-evaluate-reflections, /brana:re-evaluate-reflections, /brana:repo-cleanup, /brana:maintain-specs.

When invoked from a client or venture project (e.g. mya, lexia, linkedin) these skills are confusing or destructive — they operate on thebrana's own spec docs, not the client's.

**Goal:** gate these skills so they only activate from within thebrana (or brana-knowledge).

**Options to evaluate:**
1. **SKILL.md `project_scope` frontmatter field** — add a field like `scope: thebrana-only`. The skill loader or a PreToolUse hook checks CWD against the field before loading. If CWD is a client project, return an early-exit message: "This skill is only available from the thebrana repo."
2. **Early-exit guard block in the skill itself** — first step of the skill checks `git remote get-url origin` or `basename $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)` against an allowlist. Simpler but requires editing each skill.
3. **Skill index metadata** — add a `context` tag to SKILL.md (`context: thebrana`) and filter in the skill loader.

**Skills to scope (thebrana-only):**
- /brana:maintain-specs
- /brana:apply-errata
- /brana:re-evaluate-reflections
- /brana:repo-cleanup
- /brana:brana:maintain-specs (any spec-graph or doc 24 ops)

**Skills that should remain universal:**
- /brana:build, /brana:close, /brana:backlog, /brana:align, /brana:research, /brana:review, /brana:retrospective — these work on any project.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Running /brana:maintain-specs from clients/mya exits with a clear message instead of silently operating on wrong docs
- thebrana sessions are unaffected
- No change to universal skills
tasks.subject: Scope thebrana-only skills — gate maintain-specs, apply-errata, re-evaluate outside thebrana

tasks.description:
Sequential errata numbering in docs/24-roadmap-corrections.md is not safe under parallel sessions or worktrees. Two branches can both compute the same "next ID" before merging, producing duplicate errata numbers that require manual fix commits to untangle (happened 2026-04-14: two E142 entries).

**Fix:** Replace sequential integer IDs with timestamp-based IDs: E{YYYY-MM-DD}-{N} (e.g., E2026-04-14-1, E2026-04-14-2). Collisions become structurally impossible since each session has its own date prefix and sequential counter within the session.

**Changes required:**
- Update doc 24 table header to reflect new format
- Update close.md procedure: "next errata ID = E{today}-{count+1}"
- Update maintain-specs / apply-errata procedures
- Update MEMORY.md rule feedback_errata-numbering-vs-committed-state.md
- Migrate existing IDs? No — grandfather existing sequential IDs, new entries use timestamp format

**Acceptance criteria:**
- New errata added in a worktree do not collide with errata added concurrently in main
- All procedures reference the timestamp format
- E153 is the last sequential ID; E2026-04-14-1 would be the first timestamp ID
tasks.subject: Timestamp-based errata IDs — replace sequential E142 with E2026-0414-1 to prevent collisions

tasks.description:
The close procedure (Step 6: field notes) has a heuristic for routing learnings: "Would this be useful to someone who has never seen this repo but knows the tool? Yes → dimension doc. No → CLAUDE.md." But this is advisory, and in practice the session wrote field notes to CLAUDE.md then had to revert them (commits 52ef44b + 240c145 in the 2026-04-14 session).

**Fix:** Add a pre-write check to close.md Step 6 that explicitly blocks routing to CLAUDE.md for anything that matches:
- "Would be useful outside this repo" → dimension doc instead
- "Is a behavioral rule (always X, never Y)" → feedback_*.md instead (already Step 5)
- "Is a project-level architectural fact" → MEMORY.md instead

CLAUDE.md field notes should only receive repo-specific operational notes (e.g., "this hook resolves CWD from session context not worktree" — requires knowing brana's specific hook architecture). Add a validation step before each Write: confirm the target is the right scope.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- close.md Step 6 includes scope validation before each field note write
- CLAUDE.md Field Notes section only grows for genuinely repo-specific discoveries
- No more commit-then-revert for field notes
tasks.notes: Closed 2026-04-22: addressed at procedure level in system/procedures/close.md Step 6 (scope-based routing table + explicit 'CLAUDE.md is Layer 1 — human-authored only. Never write to it from /brana:close.' line 249). Hard gate is at hook layer (feedback-gate.sh / layer1-paths) which blocks .claude/CLAUDE.md writes in close context. Procedure + hook combo delivers the 'hard gate' the task requested.
tasks.subject: close: guard against routing field notes to CLAUDE.md — enforce heuristic as hard gate
fix(tdd-gate): add early exit for .md files before find commands to prevent timeout false-stops
[skill] align: auto-detect brainstorm repos → Foundation-only scope

tasks.context: Write the implementation spec before any code. Covers: InboxLogEntry v2 struct, InboxState v2, atomic log write strategy, IMAP session helper contract, all 5 command specs (read/archive/mark-read/search/open), poll v2 changes. Source: brainstorm 2026-04-15 + 5 rounds adversarial review. Must be written and reviewed before A (struct migration) begins.
tasks.subject: SDD spec: docs/architecture/features/inbox-v2.md

tasks.context: Add fields to InboxLogEntry: seq (u64, immutable), message_id (String), uid (u64, was u32), uidvalidity (u32), status (InboxStatus enum: Inbox|Read|Archived|Stale). Add InboxStatus enum. Update InboxState: add last_seq (u64), uidvalidity (u32). Serde compat: old log entries (missing new fields) must deserialize with defaults. Blocked by: t-1295 (spec).
tasks.subject: Migrate InboxLogEntry + InboxState structs to v2

tasks.context: Write helper fn write_log_atomic(entries: &[InboxLogEntry], path: &Path). Writes to inbox-log.tmp, then fs::rename to inbox-log.jsonl. Crash-safe — partial writes never corrupt the live log. Used by: poll v2, archive, mark-read. Blocked by: t-1296 (struct).
tasks.subject: Atomic log rewrite helper — temp+rename for inbox-log.jsonl

tasks.context: Extract shared IMAP session setup used by read/archive/mark-read. Pattern: 1) TLS connect, 2) LOGIN, 3) SELECT INBOX → get live UIDVALIDITY, 4) compare against log entries — on mismatch bulk-mark all status=inbox as Stale, print re-poll prompt, abort. 5) Return session + validated uid. SELECT must happen BEFORE uid resolution from log (not after). Blocked by: t-1296 (struct).
tasks.subject: IMAP session helper — SELECT-first, UIDVALIDITY check, bulk-stale on mismatch

tasks.context: Rewrite poll_account to: 1) Use mailparse::parse_headers instead of extract_header (fixes folded headers, RFC 2047 encoding). 2) Fetch MESSAGE-ID in FETCH command: RFC822.HEADER (FROM SUBJECT DATE MESSAGE-ID). 3) Assign seq = state.last_seq + 1 per message, increment state. 4) Store uidvalidity from SELECT response in each log entry and state. 5) Decode UTF-8 subjects/from at write time (not at search time). 6) Write via atomic log helper. 7) Add --bootstrap flag: uses UID SEARCH ALL instead of UNSEEN for first-run setup. Blocked by: t-1297 (atomic write), t-1298 (session helper).
tasks.subject: Poll v2 — mailparse headers, seq, MESSAGE-ID, UTF-8, --bootstrap

tasks.context: New subcommand. id accepts seq shorthand (#3 or 3) or message_id. Flow: 1) Use IMAP session helper (SELECT-first, UIDVALIDITY check). 2) Resolve id → uid from log. 3) Round trip 1: UID FETCH {uid} RFC822.SIZE. 4) Branch: size<=1MB → UID FETCH RFC822 (full); size>1MB → UID FETCH BODY[TEXT] (plaintext part only). --full flag bypasses size gate. 5) mailparse HTML→plaintext. 6) Print to stdout (pipeable). Show size warning for large emails. Blocked by: t-1298 (session helper).
tasks.subject: brana inbox read <id> — on-demand IMAP body fetch

tasks.context: New subcommand. Flow: 1) IMAP session helper. 2) Resolve id → uid. 3) Check CAPABILITY: has MOVE → UID MOVE {uid} [Gmail]/All Mail; no MOVE + has UIDPLUS → UID COPY + UID STORE \Deleted + UID EXPUNGE {uid}; neither → abort with clear error. 4) Atomic log rewrite: status=archived for entry. NOTE: COPY+DELETE+EXPUNGE without UIDPLUS nukes ALL \Deleted in mailbox — must use UID EXPUNGE specifically. Blocked by: t-1297 (atomic write), t-1298 (session helper).
tasks.subject: brana inbox archive <id> — IMAP MOVE with UIDPLUS fallback

tasks.context: New subcommand. Flow: 1) Collect UIDs from log: status=inbox (default) or all entries (--all). 2) IMAP session helper — one SELECT, validate UIDVALIDITY for batch. 3) UID STORE {uid-set} +FLAGS (\Seen). 4) Track per-UID OK/NO responses. 5) Atomic log rewrite: status=read ONLY for UIDs that got OK response. NO responses skipped silently (already read or expunged). No log drift. Known v1 gap: connection drop mid-batch may leave server+log out of sync — deferred to v2. Blocked by: t-1297, t-1298.
tasks.subject: brana inbox mark-read [--all] — log-scoped IMAP \Seen

tasks.context: New subcommand. Flow: 1) Read inbox-log.jsonl. 2) Filter status=inbox by default; --all includes archived/read/stale. 3) Match query (case-insensitive) against from+subject+date (all UTF-8 at rest from poll v2 — no runtime decode needed). 4) Output: seq  message_id  from  subject  date. Pipeable to read: brana inbox search kapso | awk "{print \$1}" | xargs brana inbox read. Blocked by: t-1296 (struct, for status field).
tasks.subject: brana inbox search <query> — UTF-8 grep on log with status filter

tasks.context: New subcommand. Flow: 1) Resolve seq → message_id from log. 2) Construct URL: https://mail.google.com/mail/u/{gmail_account_index}/#search/rfc822msgid:{message_id}. gmail_account_index from account config (default 0). 3) Check $DISPLAY or $WAYLAND_DISPLAY: set → xdg-open URL; headless/SSH → print URL to stdout. Document in --help: gmail_account_index must match browser session order. Blocked by: t-1296 (struct, for message_id field).
tasks.subject: brana inbox open <id> — Gmail URL via message_id, $DISPLAY fallback

tasks.context: Wire all 5 new commands: 1) Add variants to InboxCmd enum in cli.rs: Read { id: String, full: bool }, Archive { id: String }, MarkRead { all: bool }, Search { query: String, all: bool }, Open { id: String }. 2) Add dispatch arms in main.rs. 3) Update --help text for all commands including gmail_account_index note for open. Blocked by: t-1300, t-1301, t-1302, t-1303, t-1304 (all commands implemented).
tasks.subject: CLI wiring — add read/archive/mark-read/search/open to InboxCmd + main.rs

tasks.context: Test coverage for all v2 changes: 1) InboxLogEntry v2 serde roundtrip (new fields + old entry compat with defaults). 2) Atomic write: write_log_atomic success + crash simulation (tmp exists, rename fails). 3) UIDVALIDITY mismatch → all inbox entries become Stale. 4) seq immutability: poll twice, verify seq never renumbered. 5) search status filter: default returns inbox-only, --all returns everything. 6) mark-read log-on-OK-only: simulate mixed OK/NO responses, verify only OK entries get status=read. Blocked by: t-1305 (all wired).
tasks.subject: Tests — inbox-v2 struct serde, atomic write, UIDVALIDITY, search, mark-read

tasks.context:
Source incident: proyecto_anita 2026-04-17 — t-302 lost when git merge --no-ff overwrote uncommitted brana backlog add to .claude/tasks.json. Recreated as t-299.

Spec:
- PreToolUse Bash hook at ~/.claude/hooks/merge-safe-tasks-json.sh
- Trigger: regex on 'git (merge|rebase|pull)' (excl. merge-base/merge-tree/mergetool)
- Check: git status --porcelain on protected files; block if any have ' M' or 'MM' state
- Protected files config: ~/.claude/brana-config.json -> {merge_safe_files: ['.claude/tasks.json']}
- Override: BRANA_MERGE_UNSAFE=1 env var (deliberate, leaves command history footprint)
- Exit 2 (PreToolUse block) with clear message + suggested fix command
- Tests: temp-repo fixture + assert block/pass/override/non-git-skip

Where to install:
- Script: enter_thebrana/thebrana/system/hooks/merge-safe-tasks-json.sh
- Registration: thebrana bootstrap.sh adds to ~/.claude/settings.json hooks.PreToolUse[]

Future extensions:
- Add per-project protected files (e.g., proyecto_anita: .claude/event-log.json)
- Companion git pre-merge-commit hook for terminal sessions outside Claude Code (defer)
tasks.subject: [hook] merge-safe-tasks-json: block git merge/rebase/pull when .claude/tasks.json has unstaged changes

tasks.context:
## Session context

**Session:** 2026-04-18, lexia venture brainstorm (/brana:brainstorm invoked)
**Duration:** ~4h, deeply iterative
**Artifacts produced en ventures/lexia/docs/:**
- architecture/platform-shape-v0.md (~900 líneas)
- guide/build-plan-v1.md (12 días MVP)
- guide/backlog-detailed-v1.md (~350 atomic tasks)
- guide/setup-checklist-martin.md
- guide/p0-calendar-timeline.md
- research/2026-04-17-pov-v0.md
- meetings/2026-04-XX-call-opener-tomi.md
- audit/2026-04-17-end-to-end-findings.md

**Commands/skills invocados:**
- /brana:brainstorm (skill principal)
- /brana:close (2x)
- Agent brana:challenger (3 rounds, Sonnet)
- Agent brana:scout (3 parallel para research desk)
- mcp__ruflo__browser_* (audit + verify)
- AskUserQuestion (múltiples decision points)

## Chat history synthesis (relevant points)

1. Usuario pidió brainstorm del platform greenfield
2. Discutimos stack (Next.js + Supabase + Netlify + Anthropic)
3. Usuario aceptó plan
4. 3 challenger rounds cambiaron: concierge-first (rejected), timeout (Vercel→Netlify), idempotency, P0 calendar vs in-person
5. Backlog detallado con atomic tasks
6. Usuario: 'backlog add why DDD/SDD/TDD/Docs still not being enforced?'  ← aquí se destapó el gap

## Root causes identificados

### RC1: brainstorm.md tiene las reglas pero no hay enforcement
Lines 232-246 listan 'Engineering disciplines' en el Phase 4 SHAPE.
Lines 300-358 listan 'DDD/TDD/SDD/Docs tasks are mandatory when planning' con Governance Gate.

Pero el Governance Gate es un AskUserQuestion manual que depende de que Claude lo invoque. No hay hook que lo fuerce.

### RC2: Challenger agent prompt no incluye check de disciplinas
Los challenger rounds evaluaron stack, timeout, idempotency, P0 calendar — pero nunca preguntaron 'este plan respeta DDD/TDD/SDD/Docs per brana procedure?'

### RC3: /brana:backlog plan (invocado por brainstorm en Phase 5b) no automáticamente agrega ADR + test + spec + docs tasks
Si lo hace, en este caso no pasó. Verificar en system/procedures/backlog.md.

### RC4: No hay lint/gate post-plan
Un hook tipo 'PostSkillUse' que ejecute después de /brana:brainstorm y verifique que los outputs incluyen ADR tasks como blockers sería el enforcement missing.

## Propuesta de fix (high-level)

### Fix 1: Challenger agent default checklist
Agregar a system/agents/challenger.md (o equivalent) un item default: 'Does the plan/design include ADR tasks as blockers pre-impl? Tests-first per impl day? Per-feature specs? Per-feature user guides?'
Scoring rule: si cualquiera de los 4 está missing en M+ effort → score ≥3 (warning).

### Fix 2: Brainstorm Phase 5b auto-generates discipline tasks
When brainstorm invokes /brana:backlog plan, the plan should AUTO-include:
- 1 ADR task per architectural decision area → blocks impl
- 1 TDD subsection per impl day → blocks impl tasks of that day
- 1 SDD stub + 1 SDD update per feature
- 1 user guide per feature in wrap-up

No manual prompt necesario.

### Fix 3: System-wide rule (add to ~/.claude/rules/)
Crear rule 'm-plus-discipline-enforcement.md' que especifica:
'Any plan doc marked as M+ effort MUST surface DDD/TDD/SDD/Docs coverage explicitly. If a plan doc exists in docs/ without these elements, flag via doc-audit hook.'

### Fix 4: Audit hook
Hook que detecta creación de docs tipo 'build-plan*.md' o 'backlog*.md' y verifica via grep que contienen ADR tasks + test tasks + spec tasks + user guide tasks. Si no, warning ruidoso.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Fix 1 (challenger checklist) — updated en próximo update del agent
- [ ] Fix 2 (brainstorm auto-disciplines) — cambio a system/procedures/brainstorm.md o brainstorm.md + backlog.md
- [ ] Fix 3 (system rule) — nuevo rule file en system/rules/
- [ ] Fix 4 (doc-audit hook) — hook new en system/hooks/ que valida plans
- [ ] Test: re-run /brana:brainstorm con un idea M+ y verificar que auto-agrega discipline tasks al backlog sin prompt manual

## Relacionado

- Session close patterns — feedback_challenger-cut-protection-check.md (rule pattern transferable para ANY multi-round review)
- Memory file: feedback_challenger-cut-protection-check.md
- Lexia specific remediation: backlog-detailed-v1.md v1.2 con P0.ADR.*, P0.SPEC.*, DX.T.*, DX.S.*, D12.U.*

## Priority rationale

High: afecta todo venture brainstorm en brana going forward. Sin fix, cada nuevo venture repite el mismo gap y requiere usuario detectar manualmente.
tasks.description:
El procedure de /brana:brainstorm (system/procedures/brainstorm.md lines 232-358) dice explícitamente que DDD/TDD/SDD/Docs son MANDATORY para efforts M+. Pero no hay enforcement automático. El executor (Claude) puede producir un plan completo que viola la disciplina, y ningún layer detecta.

Evidencia concreta: sesión 2026-04-18 lexia brainstorm. Producido: platform-shape-v0, build-plan-v1, backlog detallado (~350 tasks), 3 challenger rounds. Ninguno incluía:
- ADRs como blocker tasks pre-impl (DDD)
- Tests-first por día (TDD) — tests estaban en Day 10 at-end
- Per-feature specs (SDD) — solo monolith platform-shape
- Per-feature user guides (Docs) — solo operational docs

El gap solo se surfaceó cuando el usuario preguntó explícitamente 'en tu plan respetaste DDD/SDD/TDD/Docs?' Un audit manual dio: DDD=no, TDD=no, SDD=parcial, Docs=parcial. Fix agregó ~65 tasks + 3-5 días calendario al plan de lexia.
tasks.subject: [system] DDD/TDD/SDD/Docs disciplines no enforced automáticamente en /brana:brainstorm ni caught por challenger

tasks.description: When Claude asks do you want me to continue with t-XXX? and the user accepts, Claude proceeds directly into implementation instead of invoking /brana:backlog start (or /brana:do). This bypasses task claiming, recipe firing, strategy classification, skill suggestion, and progress tracking. Fix: on any acceptance of a task-continuation suggestion, invoke the backlog start workflow before any implementation.

tasks.context:
Root cause: system/cli/rust/crates/brana-core/src/tasks.rs:105-108 calls classify(t, all) and compares against raw CLI enum string. classify() returns {done, active, blocked, pending, parked}; CLI enum is {pending, in_progress, completed, cancelled}. Input 'completed' can never match output 'done'. Repro: brana backlog query --status pending --stream tech-debt --output json | jq '[.[] | select(.status != "pending")] | length' returns 40 contaminated items.

Blast radius: all CLI + MCP filter_tasks callers when --status != pending. Also affects cmd_next, which passes 'pending' and depends on classify semantics to exclude blocked/parked.

Fix design: filter_tasks compares raw t.status directly. cmd_next applies post-hoc classify filter to exclude blocked/parked. This aligns CLI enum with raw field values and preserves cmd_next semantics.

Callers: cmd_next (cli backlog.rs:27), cmd_query (backlog.rs:83), cmd_search (165, None), backlog_query.rs MCP (49), backlog_search.rs MCP (20, None).

TDD 3-commit pattern: spec stub, failing tests, passing impl + updated tests (tasks.rs:1066 test_filter_by_status uses 'active' which must shift to 'in_progress' under raw semantics) + docs.
tasks.notes: Shipped 2026-04-22 in 1499dca (fix) after 938da05 (spec) + ad88956 (tests). Merged via --no-ff.
tasks.subject: [cli] fix(backlog-query): filter_tasks compares classify() output vs raw CLI status — silent contamination

tasks.context:
Root cause: resolve_lookup_dir in system/hooks/lib/git-helpers.sh only extracts 'git -C <path>' but not a leading 'cd <path> && git add|commit'. When Claude issues 'cd /worktree && git add X', shell cwd may be reset between invocations — hook input CWD comes from Claude session, not the cd target. Result: hook checks wrong repo branch.

Lived through the exact bug today fixing t-1323: the filter-fix worktree needed 'cd <worktree> && git commit' to be issued as 'git -C <worktree> commit' after hook rejection. Users (or Claude) will keep hitting this pattern.

Fix: extend extract_git_c_dir / add parse_cd_prefix helper in git-helpers.sh. resolve_lookup_dir checks -C first, falls back to parsed cd <path> &&, then CWD. Precedence: -C > cd-prefix > CWD.

Tests: unit tests in test-branch-verify.sh and test-main-guard.sh covering (a) 'cd /wt && git add X' where /wt is on feature branch → pass, (b) same where /wt is main → deny, (c) 'cd /a && git -C /b add' → -C wins, (d) quoted paths with spaces.

Closes t-1286, t-1316. Also closes t-1154 if we add a replay fixture.
tasks.subject: [hook] Extend resolve_lookup_dir to parse 'cd <path> &&' prefix for worktree-style staging (t-1286 + t-1316)
[close] Wave F close-loop polish: pending-reconcile + stash cleanup + debrief repo filter (t-1322+t-1130+t-1311)

tasks.context: Observed 2026-04-22: 5 concurrent sessions on proyecto_anita; one session wiped another pointer additions twice in ~10 min. Previous proposed fix (PreToolUse merge hook) was over-engineered — regeneration from source-of-truth files is simpler and race-free.
tasks.description:
MEMORY.md concurrent-write conflict arises when multiple CC sessions append pointer lines simultaneously — last-write-wins wipes the other session's additions. Root cause: MEMORY.md is treated as a mutable file when it should be a derived index.

Revised approach: make MEMORY.md a generated artifact. Individual memory files (feedback_*.md, project_*.md, etc.) are the source of truth — they have unique filenames so no concurrent-write race is possible. A PostToolUse hook fires after any Write to ~/.claude/projects/*/memory/*.md, scans all files in the directory, and regenerates MEMORY.md from frontmatter (name, description fields). No merge logic, no blocking, no side-files. Any session's write to an individual file triggers a clean regeneration from current disk state.

Acceptance: two concurrent session writes to different memory files both appear in MEMORY.md after both PostToolUse hooks run.
tasks.subject: MEMORY.md concurrent-write hardening — diff-merge hook for parallel sessions

tasks.context:
Hit during t-1323 TDD cycle: 'cargo test -p brana-core' failed with 'pkg-config not found' even though libssl-dev was installed. Workaround: set OPENSSL_DIR=/usr OPENSSL_LIB_DIR=/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu OPENSSL_INCLUDE_DIR=/usr/include. brana-core depends on native-tls which chains to openssl-sys which requires pkg-config. brana doctor should flag missing pkg-config before first cargo invocation. Cost: ~10min lost debugging in a session with sudo password unavailable.


---

**Extension (2026-05-03, from t-1337 follow-up):** Add a second doctor check — verify `which brana` resolves to `~/.local/bin/brana` and warn if a different binary is on PATH (e.g. nvm-linked old JS prototype at ~/brana/, which silently broke /brana:close session writes for an entire session before detection). Pattern: any auxiliary install or npm link can shadow the canonical install. One-line check: `[ "$(command -v brana)" = "$HOME/.local/bin/brana" ]` — fail if not. Closes follow-up (a) from t-1337 notes.
tasks.notes: Implemented in commit b496dbf, merged via 3-way merge. Two new checks: check_pkg_config (catches missing build dep) and check_brana_resolution (catches PATH shadows like t-1337). 5 new tests + 30 total in doctor module, all green. Smoke test verified: 8/9 checks pass on dev box (existing Plugin registered issue unrelated).
tasks.subject: [cli] brana doctor: add pkg-config pre-flight check — cargo build of brana-core requires it

tasks.context: Step 11c PENDING-RECONCILE (added this session in t-1325) matched t-030 via commit '9aa22c3 feat(t-032): migrate doc 30 pending items to tasks.json' — t-030 appears in commit body (from migration). Current filter: '^[0-9a-f]+ (fix|feat|merge)'. Needs: exclude messages matching 'migrate|triage|reprioritize|demote|promote|add t-NNN|chore\(tasks\)'. Or invert — only match 'fix\(.*t-NNN' / 'feat\(.*t-NNN' / 'merge\(.*t-NNN' where the ID is in the scope prefix, not the body. Precision > recall.
tasks.subject: [close] PENDING-RECONCILE: tighten false-positive filter — exclude add/chore/migrate/triage commits that reference IDs in body

tasks.context: Old prototype JS project at ~/brana/ (package.json: name=brana, bin=brana → src/cli/index.js) is npm-linked globally. The symlink lives at ~/.nvm/versions/node/v20.19.0/bin/brana → ~/brana. nvm bin precedes ~/.local/bin on PATH, so bare `brana session ...` resolves to the JS prototype, which has no `session` subcommand → `error: unknown command session`. Confirmed via `which brana` (returns nvm path), `~/.local/bin/brana session --help` (works), `npm ls -g brana` (shows brana@1.0.0 → ./../../../../../brana). Skills like /brana:do call bare `brana ...`; every such call hits the wrong binary. Fix applied this session: `npm rm -g brana` (removes only the nvm symlink; ~/brana/ source untouched). Followups: (1) brana doctor should detect this — check `which brana` resolves to ~/.local/bin/brana, warn otherwise; (2) decide whether to delete or rename ~/brana/ (old prototype superseded by Rust CLI in thebrana/system/cli/rust); (3) audit any other procedure invoking bare `brana` to confirm none depend on the JS prototype.

Verified: which brana → /home/martineserios/.local/bin/brana, brana --version → 0.2.0, brana session --help works. Followups: (a) brana doctor should warn if 'which brana' != ~/.local/bin/brana (raise as separate task or fold into t-1327); (b) ~/brana/ old JS prototype can be deleted/archived — superseded by Rust CLI at thebrana/system/cli/rust.

tasks.description:
All flywheel metrics (events, corrections, test_writes, correction_rate, test_write_rate, cascade_rate) stay zero across every session-state.json file. Only delegation_count occasionally non-zero.

**Diagnóstico (2026-05-06):**
1. Telemetry IS captured — /tmp/brana-session-${SESSION_ID}.jsonl files exist with 100s-1000s of events per session.
2. `brana ops metrics <session-file>` works correctly when run manually — produces full flywheel block (correction_rate, test_write_rate, etc.).
3. The session-end.sh hook flushes events to history.jsonl but does NOT update session-state.json metrics field.
4. The /brana:close procedure (close.md Step 9) says metrics are merged automatically by session-end hook — this is documentation drift, the hook does not do this.

**Evidence (batrade sessions):**
- 2026-04-29: events 0, corrections 0, ..., delegation_count 2
- 2026-05-01: events 0, corrections 0, ..., delegation_count 0  
- 2026-05-06: events 0, corrections 0, ..., delegation_count 0

**Fix recomendado: Option B — fix en session-end.sh:** después de computar métricas via `brana ops metrics`, leer session-state.json del proyecto, mergear bloque metrics, escribir atómico. Hace el fix universal a cualquier path que escribe session state, no solo /brana:close.

**Alternative Option A — fix en close.md procedure:** invocar `brana ops metrics $SESSION_FILE` durante Step 9 e inline result en payload antes de `brana session write`. Menos invasivo pero deja el bug en cualquier otra skill que escriba session state.

**Affects:** all brana clients (cross-cutting). Documentation drift in close.md says hook does the merge.

**Source:** discovered via /brana:close in batrade session 2026-05-06 cuando user preguntó por qué metrics siempre zero.
tasks.notes: Root cause: session-end.sh does cd /tmp; brana session path resolved -tmp- instead of real project. Fix: cd to GIT_ROOT before all three CLI calls in persist.sh. T9 added to test-session-end-split.sh.
tasks.subject: fix: session-end hook does not merge `brana ops metrics` into session-state.json

tasks.context: Partner on proyecto_anita needs roadmap visibility without touching brana backlog internals. Decision 2026-05-13: one-way push (brana backlog → Linear), scoped to proyecto_anita. Phases + milestones become Linear Projects/Issues. brana backlog stays canonical for thebrana + solo projects. Linear MCP (linear-server) already wired into proyecto_anita sessions.
tasks.description:
Implement `brana backlog sync --linear [--project <slug>]`.

Behavior:
- Reads phases + milestones from tasks.json for the target project
- Creates/updates Linear Team (if not exists) and Projects per phase
- Pushes milestone tasks as Linear Issues with status mapping (pending→Todo, in_progress→In Progress, completed→Done)
- Stores Linear issue IDs in tasks.json for idempotent re-sync
- Scoped to a single project slug at a time (no global sync)

Out of scope: reverse sync (Linear → brana backlog), PR/branch linking, sub-task hierarchy.
tasks.subject: [backlog] brana backlog sync --linear: one-way push phases+milestones → Linear

tasks.context: Decision 2026-05-13: Linear is source of truth for proyecto_anita manual tasks (partner writes there). Dev tasks stay in brana backlog. Need one-way sync: phases + milestones → Linear Projects + Milestones. NOT bidirectional — partner writes manual tasks directly in Linear, never in brana backlog. Two write domains are non-overlapping so no conflict resolution needed. Scope: proyecto_anita only. Output: `brana backlog sync --linear [--project proyecto_anita]` subcommand. Maps: phase→LinearProject, milestone→LinearMilestone, preserves brana IDs in Linear description for traceability. Auth: Linear API key from ~/.config/brana/linear.env or LINEAR_API_KEY env var.
tasks.subject: brana backlog sync --linear (one-way push for proyecto_anita)

tasks.context: Adopts 10 features from CC v2.1.136–v2.1.142 into the thebrana system. Shaped from brainstorm 2026-05-15. Idea doc: docs/ideas/cc-feature-adoption-v2.1.136-142.md. Three milestones: hook hardening (Tier 0+1), 1-liner bundle (Tier 1), /goal from task context (Tier 2).
tasks.subject: CC Feature Adoption — v2.1.136–v2.1.142
Hook Infrastructure Hardening

tasks.context:
Add "if" condition field to the branch-verify.sh and commit-msg-verify.sh hook entries in hooks.json. Use permission rule syntax: "if": "Bash(git *)". These hooks currently spawn on every Bash invocation — the if field makes them only spawn when the command matches git commands. Measurable latency reduction on every non-git bash call in every session.

AC: both hooks have if:"Bash(git *)" condition; verified via hooks.json diff; manual timing test shows non-git Bash calls no longer trigger these hooks.
tasks.subject: Add if pre-filters to branch-verify and commit-msg-verify hooks

tasks.context:
Migrate every hook entry in hooks.json from string command form to exec-form args[]. Old: {"type":"command","command":"bash .claude/hooks/foo.sh"}. New: {"type":"command","args":["bash",".claude/hooks/foo.sh"]}. Eliminates shell invocation — no quoting bugs, no shell injection surface. Audit each hook first: some may use shell features (pipes, env expansion) and need the string form retained with a comment.

AC: all hook entries use args[] form except those explicitly needing shell features (documented with inline comment); tests/hooks/ suite passes; no regression in hook behavior.
tasks.subject: Migrate all hook commands to exec-form args[] (eliminate shell invocation)

tasks.context:
Add a Gate Taxonomy section to docs/architecture/hooks.md. Two classes: (1) Enforcement gates — hard-stop, no continueOnBlock: TDD gate, main-guard, branch-verify, worktree-gate. (2) Advisory gates — continueOnBlock appropriate: feedback-gate, post-plan-challenge, post-tasks-validate. This taxonomy must be documented before any continueOnBlock wiring so future sessions understand why some gates recover and others do not.

AC: hooks.md has a Gate Taxonomy section; all existing gates are classified under one of the two classes; the why (blocking=signal vs feedback=loop) is explained.
tasks.subject: Document enforcement vs advisory gate taxonomy in docs/architecture/hooks.md

tasks.context:
Wire continueOnBlock:true on the three advisory gates: feedback-gate, post-plan-challenge, post-tasks-validate. When these gates block (decision:block), Claude receives the rejection reason as context and can self-correct instead of the agent loop dying. Enforcement gates (TDD, main-guard, branch-verify, worktree-gate) must NOT get continueOnBlock — they stay hard-stop. Taxonomy defined in t-1414 must be committed first.

AC: 3 advisory gate hook entries have continueOnBlock:true; test assertions explicitly check continue values for both gate types (wave transition audit per CLAUDE.md field note 2026-04-20); TDD gate and main-guard test assertions unchanged and still assert hard-stop.
tasks.subject: Add continueOnBlock to advisory gates (feedback-gate, post-plan-challenge, post-tasks-validate)
1-liner Config Bundle

tasks.context:
config-change-guard.sh already exists but is not wired to the ConfigChange hook event. Move it from its current wiring to the ConfigChange event in ~/.claude/settings.json (user-level, not project — must protect all sessions). This closes CVE-2026-21852: a PreToolUse hook that modifies ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL could redirect traffic to an untrusted endpoint.

AC: ConfigChange event in ~/.claude/settings.json invokes config-change-guard.sh; prior wiring removed or consolidated; hook fires when settings.json is modified.
tasks.subject: Wire ConfigChange hook for ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL guard

tasks.context:
Two independent 1-liner changes bundled: (1) Set MCP_CONNECTION_NONBLOCKING=1 in .mcp.json env block or session-start — CC sessions start without blocking on ruflo/brana-mcp startup. Tradeoff: tools unavailable for ~2s at session start (acceptable). (2) brana-mcp and ruflo MCP server code should read CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR env var (injected by CC since v2.1.139) instead of using heuristic CWD detection for project root resolution.

AC: sessions start without blocking; brana-mcp uses CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR when available with CWD fallback; ruflo equivalent updated.
tasks.subject: Set MCP_CONNECTION_NONBLOCKING=1 and consume CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR in brana-mcp and ruflo

tasks.context:
cc-changelog-check.sh already polls npm for new CC versions and writes ~/.claude/cc-changelog-report.md. session-start.sh already reads that report file. The only missing piece: wire cc-changelog-check.sh as an async SessionStart hook so it runs in the background on every session start. async:true means session startup is not blocked. When a new version is detected, the report file is created and session-start surfaces it in additionalContext on the next session.

AC: hooks.json has a SessionStart entry invoking cc-changelog-check.sh with async:true; verified via manual version-bump simulation (write a fake cached version, start session, confirm report appears in additionalContext).
tasks.subject: Wire cc-changelog-check.sh as async SessionStart hook
/goal from Task Context

tasks.context:
Document the AC: convention in the task-convention rule or CLAUDE.md. Convention: task context field may contain one or more lines starting with "AC:" that define machine-readable acceptance criteria. Example: "AC: all tests green, branch merged to main, tasks.json updated". These lines will be consumed by /brana:build to auto-generate a /goal string. The convention is additive — existing tasks without AC: are unaffected.

AC: task-convention rule or CLAUDE.md documents the AC: prefix convention with an example; the convention is non-breaking (no existing tasks modified).
tasks.subject: Document AC: convention for task context field

tasks.context:
Update /brana:build (system/procedures/build.md) to grep the active task context for lines starting with AC: during the LOAD step. If found, extract them and inject a /goal <criteria> call at the start of the build session. If not found, proceed normally with no regression. This ships /goal as a first-class feature driven by task data rather than human-typed strings.

AC: /brana:build reads AC: lines from task context when present; /goal is injected with the criteria text; tasks without AC: in context proceed without /goal (no regression); tested with a task that has AC: lines and one that does not.
tasks.subject: Wire /goal auto-generation into /brana:build from AC: in task context

tasks.context:
Build a web UI to monitor and operate the thebrana system. Goals: (1) real-time visibility into hook activity, session state, MCP server health, backlog progress; (2) Kanban board for tasks (drag-drop status changes, blocked chains visualized); (3) eventually replace terminal as the primary interface for day-to-day brana operation.

Shape questions to answer before building: stack (Next.js on Vercel? local Tauri app?), data source (brana-mcp as backend? direct tasks.json read?), scope of v1 (read-only dashboard vs interactive Kanban vs full operator panel), auth model (local-only vs cloud-synced).

AC (shape, not build): a proposal doc exists at docs/ideas/brana-system-ui.md with stack decision, v1 scope, and a wireframe of the Kanban view.
tasks.subject: Brana System UI — monitoring dashboard + Kanban task view

tasks.context: branch-verify.sh lines 82-88 have a reverse-direction case check that matches bare dir name "system" against behavioral subpaths via glob system/*. Hook fires on completely unrelated commits (.claude/tasks.json + docs/ideas/*) because the is_behavioral() function returns true for "system" — since system/hooks starts with system/. Even worse: the hook scans the full Bash command text for the pattern git-space-add, so a brana backlog add call with the word git-add anywhere in its JSON payload ALSO triggers the hook. Fix: remove the reverse-direction case entirely (parent-dir staging is not a real attack vector; actual blobs from staging always have full paths). Also add regression test asserting .claude/tasks.json + docs/ideas/foo.md pass on main. AC: is_behavioral("system") returns false; hook message includes expanded path when reverse-dir match does fire.
tasks.subject: fix(branch-verify): remove reverse-direction parent-dir match causing false positives

tasks.context: fast-cold-start.md line 42 claims MCP_CONNECTION_NONBLOCKING=true only works in -p (headless) mode. cc-feature-adoption doc recommends setting it unconditionally. t-1418 set it in .mcp.json. tasks.subject: Verify MCP_CONNECTION_NONBLOCKING works in interactive mode (E2026-05-17-1)

tasks.subject: Document hook entry schema: settings.json command-string vs hooks.json args[] (E2026-05-17-2)

tasks.subject: Fix brana reference generate: script names blank after async hook entry added

tasks.context: t-1428 revealed that hooks.json schema can drift from reference.rs without detection. Adding --check run to validate.sh (similar to existing checks) would catch this at commit time. Command: brana reference generate --check. Fail if any .md would change.
tasks.subject: Add brana reference generate --check to validate.sh

tasks.context: Session t-1429 revealed validate.sh for-loops over globs (e.g. system/agents/*.md) exit the script via set -e when no files exist — unmatched glob becomes a literal path, awk fails, script exits before reaching deep checks. shopt -s nullglob at top of validate.sh would make unmatched globs expand to nothing instead.

tasks.description:
Design and implement a standard system for logging events (meetings, calls, Slack decisions, WhatsApp transcripts) so they are retrievable as context in future sessions.

Problem: events are scattered across projects with no standard schema. No convention for what goes where, no context wiring into CLAUDE.md.

Scope (full system):
1. Convention + schema — location rules, frontmatter (date, counterparty, kind, decisions[], open-items[]), naming convention
2. Context wiring — @-include rules for active counterparty logs in CLAUDE.md
3. brana:log integration — validate skill works for project-level events, test bulk WhatsApp dump ingestion
4. Migration — apply schema retroactively to existing scattered logs

AC: new event captured in <2 min, Claude auto-loads active counterparty logs, brana:log tested end-to-end, README updated with full convention.

Trigger: docs/meetings/ created in proyecto_anita 2026-05-18 — pattern should be codified at brana level.
tasks.subject: Event log system — convention, schema, context wiring, brana:log integration

tasks.context:
DESIGN UPDATED 2026-05-19 (brainstorm docs/ideas/build-skill-gate-hardening.md).

Gate condition in system/procedures/build.md step 4a is 'If NO skills results returned' — satisfied by adjacent-domain ruflo hits. Hit in t-1466: memory taxonomy patterns satisfied the gate, rust-skills was never loaded, agent wrote 469 lines of Rust tests without domain skill.

REFINED DESIGN (supersedes domain-mapping-table approach):
Replace heuristic with deterministic 3-signal detection chain + tech-aware result validation.

Signal 1: Task description + explicit tags → tech keywords ("Rust", "[rust]", "async", ".rs")
Signal 2: Project manifest files — Cargo.toml → Rust, pyproject.toml/uv.lock → Python, package.json+tsconfig.json → TypeScript
Signal 3: File paths in task description/context — extract extensions → tech inference

Skill matching: scan installed SKILL.md files for keywords overlap with detected tech terms. No new field needed — keywords already populated across all code-strategy skills (audit 2026-05-19: 100% coverage, caveman is the only missing-keywords skill and it's excluded by design).

Tech-aware ruflo check: after matching skill via keywords, check if any ruflo result key overlaps with that skill's keywords list. No overlap = mandatory ask. User skip = append 'skill-gap-warning: {skill} available but not loaded' to task context.

REMOVED: sibling task tags as detection signal (too fragile, human-authored, ambiguous scope).
REMOVED: confidence threshold change (0.3 → 0.4) — threshold serves LOAD breadth; gate uses type-relevance not confidence.

SUNSET: this procedure patch is interim. Remove steps 4 and 4a entirely when Skill Registry (t-608) ships. Add SUNSET comment to build.md.

Errata E2026-05-19-4. See field-note_skill-load-domain-match.md in auto-memory.
tasks.subject: Harden build.md step 4a: 3-signal detection + SKILL.md keywords gate (replaces domain-mapping table)

tasks.context:
Enforcement-layer complement to the build.md step 4a hardening (see sibling task). When a Write or Edit targets a *.rs file and brana:rust-skills has not been invoked this session, the PreToolUse hook should block with a hint: "Load brana:rust-skills first (step 4a of build.md)." Implementation approach: (1) session-start hook writes a session-skills.txt marker when a known skill is loaded; (2) rust-guard.sh checks for rust-skills entry in that marker before passing *.rs writes; (3) sentinel pattern (/tmp/brana-rust-skills-loaded) for lightweight check. Pair with the build.md step 4a text fix. Errata E2026-05-19-4/6. Rule: procedure text alone fails — unconditional hook enforcement closes the gap (feedback_layer1-hook-enforcement).
skill_suggested: brana:rust-skills (score: 0.50) — selected by user for context on what the hook is protecting

Hint: "Load brana:rust-skills first (step 4a of build.md)." Enforcement-layer complement to the build.md step 4a advisory gate (t-1479).

tasks.context:
t-1461 tracks same-day parallel close overwrite at write time. This task fixes the read side: sitrep Source 4 should also call brana session history --limit 3 and parse same-day entries for their next[] items (text parse or MCP mcp__brana__session_history), merging with the primary session read. Without this, first-of-day closes lose their next[] items permanently from sitrep visibility. Filed 2026-05-19 user report.
Three-option analysis (2026-05-19):
1. Fix sitrep (short-term): brana session history --limit 3, parse text output for same-day sessions, merge next[] items — no structural change to write path
2. Fix at close (t-1461): prevent overwrite — append to multi-session-per-day format (session-state-{date}-{N}.json or similar)
3. Both: t-1461 is structural fix; sitrep reads history as belt-and-suspenders
Option 3 preferred. t-1491 implements option 1 (read side); t-1461 implements option 2 (write side).

tasks.context:
Current patterns.md design has wrong incentives: bulk-extract at close produces mixed-quality signal, quarantine entries never graduate to proven, chronological pruning removes knowledge before validation, cap creates maintenance overhead. Proposed redesign: (1) patterns.md becomes 10-entry rolling window (auto-rotated per session, no cap, no manual pruning, graceful-degradation only); (2) high-value patterns promoted explicitly to feedback_*.md → MEMORY.md (always-loaded tier) when they recur 2+ sessions (3-repeat rule); (3) ruflo query in sitrep Source 6 made more targeted (specific domain query not generic task-subject+branch). Root: value IS real but implementation wrong — MEMORY.md+feedback_*.md path is the right model, patterns.md should mirror it or be retired. Coordinate with t-608 (Skill Registry) knowledge architecture pass. Discussed 2026-05-19.
Idea doc: docs/ideas/memory-pattern-learning-redesign.md. Brainstormed 2026-05-19 — full 4-component design (quality filter gate, per-pattern files + ruflo dual-write, recurrence promotion path, targeted sitrep recall). Implements resilient-pattern-store.md design. Phased P0→P3. No backlog tasks yet — user deferred planning.
tasks.notes: P0 shipped 2026-05-20. Key challenger finding: auto-extracted patterns must NOT go to MEMORY.md (always-loaded tier). Tier model: pattern_*.md (git-durable) → ruflo semantic search → MEMORY.md (human-promoted only). Residual ref sweep needed 2nd commit. P1 (recurrence detection) and P2 (targeted sitrep recall) remain as follow-ups.

tasks.context:
Right now sitrep gives a global "where am I" snapshot. With the new initiative model (Waves 1-5: initiative field, work_type, set-active subcommand, focus boosting), there's a natural hook to make sitrep initiative-aware.

Use cases:
- `/brana:sitrep backlog-redesign` → show active tasks in that initiative, last WIP commit touching them, open errata, next unblocked step
- `/brana:sitrep` with active_initiative set → auto-scope to that initiative (same as focus does for task ranking)
- `/brana:sitrep --topic` → accept an idea doc path or tag as a topic anchor, query tasks tagged/linked to it

Implementation sketch:
1. If args or active_initiative is set: `backlog_focus(initiative: X)` instead of full portfolio snapshot
2. Pull last WIP/close commit message touching files in that initiative's scope
3. Show open errata tagged with initiative slug (if any)
4. Surface next unblocked task from that initiative

Pairs naturally with: t-series that introduced initiative model (Waves 1-5, ~2026-05-19)
tasks.description: Extend /brana:sitrep so it can be scoped to an initiative, idea doc, or tag group — producing a context-recovery view for that slice of the backlog instead of the whole portfolio.

tasks.context: Decision: work_type=ops is too broad (242 tasks). tasks.context: Decision: type field has dual role (hierarchy + classification) — split into two. level = explicit hierarchy position (initiative/phase/milestone/task/subtask), even though derivable from ID prefix — explicit aids queries and automated filling. kind = classification only (feature/fix/refactor/research/docs/ops). type field retired (deprecated alias during transition). Migration: all 1413 tasks get level inferred from ID prefix; 7 classification-typed tasks get kind set from old type value; remaining tasks get kind inferred from work_type + tags. tasks.context:
In brana-core/src/session.rs merge_states(), the line `merged.consumed_at = new.consumed_at.clone().or_else(|| existing.consumed_at.clone())` bleeds the existing consumed_at into the merged state. cmd_session_write always sets consumed_at=None before writing, so the existing consumed_at from a prior session-start handshake survives into the merged output, causing sitrep to skip new items. Fix: set `merged.consumed_at = None` unconditionally in the same-day merge path. TDD: write a failing test in tests/ covering the case where existing has consumed_at=Some(timestamp) and new has consumed_at=None — the merged result must have consumed_at=None.


Fixture spec (from debrief-analyst 2026-05-22): Write a fixture-based test in system/cli/rust/crates/brana-cli/tests/ that constructs two SessionState values, calls merge_states(), and asserts:
(a) accomplished/learnings dedup preserves order (existing items first, new items appended)
(b) next[].text dedup — same text in both → only one survives
(c) consumed_at is None after merge regardless of what existing.consumed_at held
(d) metrics sum correctly (events, corrections, test_writes)
(e) session_label merges with ' | ' separator only when neither string contains the other
This fixture must be committed BEFORE the merge_states() implementation is touched in the main branch (per TDD gate).
tasks.notes: Resolved in t-1637 reconciliation commit 98b9333 — merge_states() in brana-core enforces consumed_at=None invariant; 6 fixture tests added.
tasks.subject: Fix consumed_at bleed-through in merge_states (TDD: failing test first)
