tasks.context:
write_state() in brana-core/src/session.rs needs to extract the epic slug from the current branch name and derive the session state filename: session-state-{epic-slug}.json. Extraction must use a regex match against the full branch convention pattern `^([a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]+)/(feat|fix|chore|research|test|docs|refactor)/t-[0-9]+-` — NOT naive cut -d'/' -f1, which returns 'feat' for old-style feat/t-NNN branches. On regex miss (main, orphan, old-style branch): emit a warning to stderr ("branch does not match epic convention, falling back to session-state.json") and write to session-state.json as before.

If mark_consumed() stamps the wrong file, sitrep sees the new session as unconsumed permanently — same class of bug this task is fixing.

Extract a shared fn `epic_scoped_state_path(root: &Path, branch: &str) -> PathBuf` used by all three callers.

DoD: unit tests covering (a) conforming branch → session-state-{slug}.json, (b) old-style branch feat/t-123-foo → session-state.json + stderr warning, (c) detached HEAD → fallback, (d) main → fallback. mark_consumed() test: verify it stamps the same file path that write_state() would produce for the same branch. tasks.description: Run quality comparison protocol before Phase 4 is marked done. Protocol: (1) pick one repeatable task type (e.g. competitor API scorecard), (2) run 3x with ENRICH disabled, record outputs, (3) run 3 delegations with ENRICH enabled to seed ruflo, (4) run same task 3x with ENRICH enabled, record outputs, (5) score all 6 on three criteria 0-2 each: brana convention alignment, specificity to project context, absence of generic filler, (6) pass condition: ENRICH-enabled mean ≥ ENRICH-disabled mean + 1 point. If not met, Phase 4 does not ship. Retrieval presence alone is not sufficient validation. Blocked by ENRICH (t-1629) and PERSIST (t-1631) tasks.
tasks.subject: [gemini] Compounding loop validation — quality comparison ENRICH-off vs ENRICH-on

tasks.context:
Add --all flag to `brana session read`. When set: collect session files as follows:
1. Always include session-state.json explicitly (fallback file — main-branch closes and non-conforming branches land here)
2. Glob session-state-*.json for all epic-scoped files
3. Deduplicate (session-state.json does not match the glob, but be explicit)
4. Parse each; filter to entries whose closed_at is within the last 30 days (default) or --since YYYY-MM-DD if supplied
5. Sort by closed_at descending
6. Render one block per file:
   - Epic-scoped files: header = epic slug (stripped from filename: remove 'session-state-' prefix + '.json' suffix)
   - session-state.json: header = "(orphan)" — covers main-branch closes and pre-convention branches during transition

This means --all is permanently correct, not just during transition: main-branch closes are always visible under the orphan block.

Output format: same render_text() structure per block, prefixed with the header. JSON flag: return array of {epic, state} objects where epic = slug string or "(orphan)".

Depends on ms-003 (t-1626) being merged — --all is only useful after epic-scoped writes are in place. Specifically depends on t-1637 (session.rs reconciliation) and t-1630 (epic_scoped_state_path fn) being merged first.

tasks.context:
The two session implementations have diverged and must be reconciled before any Phase 3 work begins. Editing the wrong file produces passing tests with no behavior change for users.

Known divergences:
- write_state(): brana-core calls state.clone().sanitize() and merge_states().sanitize(); brana-cli does not call sanitize()
- rotate_history() cutoff: 365 days in brana-core, 30 days in brana-cli
- current_branch(): both have their own copy

Decision to make: brana-core is the authoritative library crate. brana-cli/src/commands/session.rs should be a thin command layer that delegates to brana-core functions — it should not reimplement session logic.

Steps:
1. Audit every fn in brana-cli/src/commands/session.rs against brana-core/src/session.rs — list duplicates
2. For each duplicate: does brana-cli version have behavior the core version lacks? If yes, merge up to core first. If no, delete CLI version and call core directly.
3. Resolve rotate_history cutoff discrepancy (30d vs 365d) — pick one, document the choice, update both to match before deleting the duplicate
4. After reconciliation: brana-cli session commands call brana-core fns; no session logic lives in brana-cli

DoD: `grep -n "fn write_state\|fn read_state\|fn merge_states\|fn rotate_history\|fn current_branch\|fn mark_consumed" system/cli/rust/crates/brana-cli/src/commands/session.rs` returns zero results (or only thin wrappers with no logic). All existing session tests pass. This task is a hard prerequisite for t-1628, t-1630, t-1632.
tasks.subject: Reconcile brana-core/session.rs vs brana-cli/session.rs — designate single authoritative implementation

tasks.context:
Challenger-reviewed 2026-05-25. Full design in docs/ideas/session-continuity-multi-session.md §Fix C.

KEY DECISIONS:
1. Tier 1 scoped HERE — backlog start must write session-start marker in same PR (not a separate task)
2. File layout: session_initiative.rs (new) + session_common.rs (extract write_atomic from session.rs). #[serde(default)] on all fields, validate() before write, atomic write via shared utility.
3. Tier 2: 3 parallel signals — (2a) in_progress task scan, (2b) git→task→initiative, (2c) branch name. Exactly 1 unique = silent. 0 or 2+ = AskUserQuestion.
4. next[] pruning: Pass 1 (task_id-linked): query backlog status — completed/cancelled → resolved[]. Pass 2 (text-only): LLM judgment at close time against session accomplished + git log → resolved[] with note. No TTL. No text matching.
5. resolved[] array: pruned items move here with resolution note (not deleted — system knows what it did).
6. Archive v1: manual step in close when 100% tasks done. brana backlog complete --initiative X is a follow-up task (not a blocker).

Dependency order: ADR (schema: SessionState.initiative + InitiativeAccumulator struct) → TDD (upsert/merge/prune/resolve tests) → impl → close.md (Tier 1/2/3 detection + Step 9 write + Pass 2 LLM pruning) → sitrep.md (read initiative file first).
blocked_by: t-1461 (same-day merge prerequisite)

tasks.context:
Spike t-1509 recommends Option A: CLI shell-out. Measured P95 ~40ms (17ms avg), well under 300ms threshold from t-1502 milestone. Write ADR-045 at docs/architecture/decisions/ADR-045-backlog-ui-transport.md.

Decision: Next.js API routes call brana CLI via execFile (argv array, no shell interpolation). Read: brana backlog query --json / brana backlog get {id}. Write: brana backlog set / add.

Rejected: brana-mcp (stdio-only), axum sidecar (1-2 weeks Rust infra, no latency problem to solve).

ADR must include: latency evidence (17ms avg, 5-call board load 89ms), injection mitigation (execFile not exec), V1 scope (local-only, no auth), and Non-Actions (no axum sidecar, no WebSockets in V1).

AC: ADR-045 file committed to docs/architecture/decisions/
AC: Status = Accepted
AC: Rejected alternatives section covers brana-mcp + axum sidecar
AC: Latency evidence cited from t-1509 spike measurements
tasks.subject: [adr] ADR-045: backlog web UI transport — CLI shell-out vs axum sidecar

tasks.context:
t-1552 spike (2026-05-27) found two blockers after ControllerRegistry bug fixed in ruflo 3.10.3:
1. Cold-start: first smart:true call takes ~3.3s (7x over 500ms threshold). SmartRetrieval initializes query expansion models lazily on first use. Fix: add a warmup call at MCP server startup, or find a ruflo config to eager-load.
2. MMR over-diversification: top-2/3 results collapse to <0.1 similarity vs 0.38+ with smart:false. build.md LOAD reads all 3 results — noise in positions 2/3 pollutes context. Fix: tune MMR diversity param (if exposed by ruflo) or limit smart:true to limit:1 queries only.
Baseline smart:false: p50 199ms, p95 303ms, top-3 sim consistently 0.38-0.66. Smart:true warm: 300-330ms (acceptable), but quality worse.
Blocked by: need to confirm ruflo exposes MMR diversity param (check ruflo 3.10.3 memory_search schema).

tasks.context: Check every skill (system/skills/*/SKILL.md), agent (system/agents/*.md), and command (system/commands/*.md) for their model field. Goal: (1) downgrade to claude-haiku-4-5 wherever Sonnet/Opus is used but the task is lightweight enough for Haiku (e.g. simple query, fast ops, read-only agents); (2) identify where Gemini via agy_delegate could replace or supplement Sonnet calls (e.g. research phases, knowledge retrieval). Candidate Haiku agents: memory-curator, client-scanner, venture-scanner, scout, archiver, daily-ops, metrics-collector, pipeline-tracker. Candidate agy slots: build.md LOAD phase (already uses agy), research.md Phase 0b (already migrated), challenge.md step 4b (already migrated). Look for remaining NLM/Sonnet calls that could route to agy. Output: a mapping of current model → recommended model per skill/agent/command, plus a list of agy migration candidates not yet wired.
tasks.notes:
## Audit Results (2026-06-02)

### Model field mapping — current → recommended

**Agents (13 total):**
- sonnet → sonnet ✓: challenger, build-evaluator, debrief-analyst, pr-reviewer (all require synthesis/judgment — correct)
- haiku → haiku ✓: client-scanner, archiver, pipeline-tracker, venture-scanner, daily-ops, metrics-collector, scout, memory-curator, gemini (all lightweight — correct)
- **No downgrades needed.**

**Skills (33 total):**
- haiku → haiku ✓: acquire-skills, cargo-machete, client-retire, discover, do, export-pdf, gemini, gsheets, log, mcp-builder, memory, meta-templates, onboard, plugin, retrospective, rust-skills, scheduler, sitrep, verify-docs (19 skills)
- sonnet → sonnet ✓: align, backlog, brainstorm, build, challenge, claudemd, close, decide, docs, fix, reconcile, research, review, ship (14 skills — each requires multi-step reasoning)
- **No downgrades needed.**

**Commands:** repo-cleanup.md has no model field (correct — runs in main session).

### agy_delegate wiring — before this task

| Location | Status |
|----------|--------|
| research.md Phase 0b | ✓ wired |
| challenge.md step 4b | ✓ wired |
| gemini.md | ✓ wired |
| build.md LOAD step 2b | ✗ gap |
| brainstorm.md LOAD step 2b | ✗ gap (delegates to build.md) |

### Changes made

1. **build.md** — ToolSearch preamble: added `mcp__brana__agy_delegate`
2. **build.md LOAD step 2b** — graph-neighbor docs >100 lines now use agy_delegate for full-doc targeted extraction instead of `head -50`
3. **brainstorm.md** — ToolSearch preamble: added `mcp__brana__agy_delegate` (step 2b already delegates to build.md pattern)
tasks.subject: audit skills/agents/commands model field — downgrade to Haiku where sufficient, wire Gemini (agy) where applicable

This makes dim enrichment sessions push-capable, not just pull-capable. Discovered during t-620 (dim 46 enrich) + discussion about knowledge pipeline architecture 2026-05-27.

tasks.subject: Implement Router-as-Haiku + Worker-as-Sonnet/Opus cost routing in brana:build/execute

tasks.context: Anthropic principle: every harness component encodes assumptions about model limitations — re-evaluate after model upgrades to strip load-bearing components. Opus 4.7 ships better long-context coherence (less context anxiety), implicit multi-step reasoning, and Managed Agents with Auto Dream. Audit brana for: (1) hooks that work around model limitations now fixed, (2) context-injection logic that models handle natively, (3) scaffolding that sprint decomposition or SPECIFY step adds but Opus 4.7 no longer needs. Source: dim-46 §2.8 + Anthropic engineering harness design post.
tasks.subject: Harness simplification audit — what brana components can Claude 4 replace?

tasks.context: SuperClaude has 7 auto-detecting behavioral modes: brainstorm, orchestration, token-efficiency, etc. Brana equivalent: detect from task metadata (work_type, kind, tags) and adjust skill behavior — e.g. token-efficiency mode skips LOAD ruflo calls, uses shorter prompts; brainstorm mode enables divergent search; orchestration mode spawns subagents. Would require changes to build.md CLASSIFY step. P3 because brana already routes via work_type — this is a refinement, not a gap. Source: dim-46 §6.3 Could Adopt from SuperClaude.
tasks.subject: Design behavioral modes — auto-detect task type, switch skill behavior (brainstorm vs build vs token-efficiency)

tasks.context: Auto Dream (CC production, 2026) triggers after 24h + ≥5 sessions and runs: contradiction removal, relative→absolute date normalization, dedup, file consolidation — producing 5-15% denser memory. Brana has memory-decay scheduler job (prunes old entries) but no consolidation job. Gap: /brana:close does this manually. Feature: brana-scheduler job that (1) checks threshold (24h + N sessions since last consolidation), (2) runs dedup across ~/.claude/projects/*/memory/ files, (3) normalizes dates, (4) removes contradictions flagged by previous debrief-analyst runs, (5) writes a consolidation log. Distinct from memory-decay (which prunes by age) — this restructures live memory. See dim-46 §2.5 Auto Dream + scheduler pattern at system/scheduler/.
tasks.notes: Cancelled after premortem (2026-06-03): date normalization has zero targets in live corpus, only matches are inside URLs (would corrupt them). lint-heal.sh already covers dedup/contradiction/imputation. Auto Dream is a CC built-in already running — no implementation needed. Run t-653 first to understand Auto Dream scope before any consolidation work.
tasks.subject: Scheduled memory consolidation job — Auto Dream equivalent for brana memory files

tasks.context: Anthropic three-agent harness: Planner→Generator→Evaluator. Brana has challenger (evaluates plans) and pr-reviewer (evaluates code diffs) but nothing that evaluates the BUILD OUTCOME against the original acceptance criteria written in SPECIFY. Gap: a build could pass all tests but miss the AC intent. Feature: new agent (evaluator or build-critic) that fires at CLOSE step — reads the SPECIFY AC from the task context, reads the completed implementation, grades each AC line as met/partial/missed with concrete evidence. Distinct from pr-reviewer (code quality) and challenger (plan). Source: dim-46 §2.4 three-agent harness + Anthropic engineering post. Agent file: system/agents/build-evaluator.md.
tasks.subject: Evaluator-after-build agent — grade build output against original acceptance criteria

tasks.context: Codex/review-to-guard pattern (dim-46 §11): every recurring agent error becomes a linter rule or hook gate — not just a one-time fix. Brana has /brana:fix for errata but no upgrade path. Feature: add an upgrade step to /brana:fix procedure — after fixing a recurring errata (same class appears 2+ times in CLAUDE.md field notes), offer via AskUserQuestion: Convert to hook gate? If yes: (1) draft PreToolUse hook rule from the errata pattern, (2) propose hook script in system/scripts/, (3) add to hooks.json, (4) write a test. Automates the discipline of turning systemic errors into structural prevention. Trigger: /brana:fix detects errata ID matches a previous errata class.
tasks.subject: Errata-to-hook flow — /brana:fix upgrade converts recurring errata into PreToolUse gate

tasks.context: RTK (Rust Token Killer, 55K stars) achieves 80-92% CLI output compression via TOML filter rules with <10ms overhead. Brana already has context-budget rules but no PostToolUse compression for Bash output. Feature: PostToolUse hook on Bash tool calls that applies brana-specific filter rules to common verbose outputs before they land in context — e.g. cargo test output (strip timing lines, keep failures), brana backlog roadmap (strip completed tasks), git log (strip hashes, keep subjects). Implement as: (1) evaluate whether RTK binary integration is cleanest, or (2) native bash filter in a hook script with TOML-like config. Start with 3-5 high-value patterns. Audit t-1703 (RTK vs context-budget hooks) first to avoid duplication. Source: dim-46 §2.8 + RTK ecosystem.
tasks.subject: PostToolUse Bash output compression hook — RTK-style context trimming for verbose CLI output

tasks.context: system/agents/CALIBRATION.md has type:reference in its frontmatter but the pre-commit budget script (system/scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit:109-113) counts ALL .md files in agents/ including type:reference files. CALIBRATION.md contributes 105 bytes to the budget even though it is not a real agent. Fix: in the agent descriptions loop, skip files where frontmatter contains type:reference. Grep: sed -n 1,5p system/agents/CALIBRATION.md. Budget was at 28671/28672 after adding gemini.md — 1 byte under limit. Excluding CALIBRATION.md would give 28671-105=28566, restoring headroom for future agents.
tasks.subject: fix(budget): pre-commit context budget should skip type:reference agent files

Pre-existing before t-1498 — all 331 tests pass on main. Fix: read_state should use state.branch for path resolution, not current_branch().

tasks.context: Conversation 2026-05-30: explored where agy is used (research Phase 0b, challenge step 4b, gemini skill). Identified a gap: agy is absent from backlog and build. Build exclusion is correct — implementation output goes to repo and delegation adds a re-review round-trip with no gain. Backlog is different: agy writing task mutations is wrong (authoritative state, corruption risk), but agy DRAFTING task content (context, descriptions) that Claude reviews and submits via CLI is a valid pattern. Concrete use case: decomposing a large epic into 5+ tasks — today Claude writes each context inline, agy could batch-draft all in parallel, Claude reviews, then brana backlog add. This task is the brainstorm path: deep analysis of whether this is actually worth building, what the UX looks like, how it integrates with the backlog procedure, and whether the latency/complexity tradeoff is positive.
tasks.subject: brainstorm: agy batch context drafting for backlog tasks

tasks.context: Conversation 2026-05-30: audited agy usage across all skills. /brana:close is the weak link — it only passively checks scheduler output files (ls system/scheduler/outputs/*.md). If no overnight sweep ran, agy contributes nothing to the close. research and challenge both actively call mcp__brana__agy_delegate by default with two-pass retrieval. Question: should close add an active agy call — e.g. querying for pattern candidates from the session before debrief-analyst runs — so close gets agy enrichment regardless of whether a scheduler sweep happened? Brainstorm the use case, prompt design, and whether it would actually improve close output quality vs adding latency.
tasks.subject: brainstorm: active agy call in /brana:close

tasks.context: Conversation 2026-05-30: audited model selection across all skills and agents. brainstorm is on sonnet — the only reasoning-heavy skill not using opus. Deep idea maturation + strategic analysis is exactly the use case where Opus 4.8 quality compounds over Sonnet 4.6. All other creative/analytical depth tasks (challenge) already use Opus internally. Fix: change model: sonnet to model: opus in system/skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md. Aliases are version-agnostic so this auto-picks Opus 4.8. Also check review.md stale label (Opus 4.6 vs Sonnet 4.6 vs Haiku) and update to current family names.
tasks.subject: bump /brana:brainstorm model from sonnet to opus

tasks.context:
ruflo exposes ~95 tools across: hive-mind (10), swarm (4), agent spawn/manage/wasm/DAA (~37), coordination (7), workflow (12), task/job (9), claims/work-stealing (12), progress (4). Goal: map which of these complement our existing skills+hooks+agy system and identify concrete leverage points.

Key questions to answer:
1. hive-mind vs CC Workflow tool — when to use each? ruflo hive-mind_spawn vs CC Agent()? Overlap vs complement?
2. Can ruflo workflow_* replace or augment the CC Workflow harness for long-running jobs?
3. claims + work-stealing — useful for multi-session task handoff? Pairs with brana session continuity?
4. DAA (dynamic adaptive agents) — does daa_agent_adapt add anything over CC agent reruns?
5. coordination_orchestrate + coordination_load_balance — useful for brana skill fan-outs?
6. progress_watch — can replace or complement Monitor tool for background jobs?
7. agy (Gemini Flash via brana-mcp) + ruflo managed_agent — two-tier delegation: ruflo for orchestration shell, agy for cheap LLM subtasks?
8. WASM agents — sandboxed execution for untrusted skill logic?

Desired output: integration map (keep / replace / complement / skip per tool group) + 2-3 concrete implementation proposals ranked by leverage.
Output: docs/ideas/ruflo-orchestration-integration.md (2026-05-30). Produces: integration map (11 tool groups, tier/verdict/fallback), ToolSearch preamble spec, hive-mind quorum 4-gate spec, validate.sh Check 36.
tasks.subject: Analyze ruflo orchestration surface — design integration with skills, agy, and CC agents/workflows

tasks.description: Wire the full auto-loop: (1) /brana:build Step 0 reads acceptance_criteria from active task via brana backlog get; (2) if criteria present, emit /goal "<task subject> — Done when: <criteria joined with AND>" before build starts; (3) Stop hook reads /goal exit status — if criteria all met, call brana backlog set t-XXX status completed automatically; (4) if criteria not met, surface which items failed, do not auto-cancel. Blocked by t-1777 (schema impl must exist). This closes the loop: human writes criteria once, build self-terminates correctly.

Then bulk-cancel via backlog-reconcile.sh --execute (scoped to URL stubs only — the 75 non-URL stubs are actual planned features and must NOT be cancelled). Batch data in /tmp/reconcile-stubs.jsonl (may need regeneration). IDs: t-116,t-117,t-119 to t-126,t-127 to t-157,t-328,t-329,t-333,t-336,t-338,t-339,t-341,t-343,t-344,t-345,t-650,t-652. tasks.context: Add 'brana knowledge process-url <url>' CLI subcommand that uses the ruflo browser agent as the fetch substrate for JS-heavy or auth-gated URLs (LinkedIn, Notion, etc.). Flow: 1) try WebFetch first (fast, works for public pages + LinkedIn with crawler rendering); 2) fall back to ruflo browser_open → browser_click dismiss-modal → browser_snapshot if WebFetch returns auth wall or empty content; 3) extract insight via agy_delegate or local LLM summarization; 4) store to ruflo memory (namespace=knowledge, key=slugified-url, tags=[domain, topic]); 5) return stored key + insight summary. Batch variant: 'brana knowledge process-url --file tasks.jsonl' reads id+url pairs, processes in sequence, outputs cancellation list. Primary use case: drain LinkedIn research stubs from backlog (validated in session 2026-05-30 — t-1780 is the first consumer). Ruflo browser session pattern validated: browser_open → click e1 (Dismiss modal) → snapshot gets full post text.
tasks.subject: [cli] brana knowledge process-url — fetch URL via ruflo browser agent, extract insight, store to ruflo

tasks.context:
AskUserQuestion throws InputValidationError when any option is missing the required `description` field. Recurs because procedures use shorthand option syntax (label only, no description) for Skip/Archive/etc options. Example: close.md field-notes multiselect had `{"label": "Skip all"}` with no description — errored at runtime.

Fix scope:
1. Audit all system/procedures/*.md and system/skills/**/*.md for AskUserQuestion calls
2. Find every option object missing a `description` key
3. Add minimal descriptions (e.g. `"description": "No field notes this session."` for Skip options)

Priority P1: blocks close and field-note workflows at runtime with no graceful fallback — user sees a hard error mid-procedure.

Heuristic grep to find candidates: grep -rn for AskUserQuestion blocks where an option line has no following description: sub-line. Initial scan found 15+ hits in brainstorm.md alone — likely more across all procedures.

Ref: E2026-05-31 session, close.md field-notes step.
tasks.subject: [fix] AskUserQuestion options missing description field — recurring InputValidationError

tasks.context:
Feed pipeline (feed-poll, feed-summarize, feed-index, feed-ruflo-index) currently runs on local XPS 13 via systemd user timers. Linger=yes keeps it alive but jobs are missed whenever the laptop is asleep at scheduled times (18:55 feed-summarize is the most sensitive — claude -p needs network). Fix: move the 4 feed jobs to an always-on box.

Options (in order of preference):
1. Oracle Cloud Free Tier ARM instance — already used for brana transcriber (t-581). SSH key already set up. Re-use the same instance: deploy brana-scheduler there, copy scheduler.json feed jobs, set CLAUDE env. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY on the server for claude -p calls.
2. Any other always-on VPS or home server.

AC: feed-poll, feed-summarize, feed-index, feed-ruflo-index all running on remote host
AC: feed-summaries.jsonl and intelligence-feed-digest.md synced back to local machine (via sync-state.sh or rsync in session-start hook)
AC: local laptop scheduler.json feed jobs disabled (enabled: false) to avoid double-runs
AC: claude -p on remote server confirmed working with API key
tasks.subject: Migrate feed pipeline to always-on server (Oracle Cloud / VPS)

tasks.context:
FINDINGS (2026-06-03):
1. /workflows = live progress monitor CLI command. Shows phase trees, agent counts, progress tree in real time.
2. Dynamic = inline `script` JS param to Workflow tool. Saved = named files in .claude/workflows/ (invoked with `name:`). scriptPath = edit file then re-invoke — enables iterative workflow dev without resending full script.
3. Brana skills CAN invoke Workflow tool — a skill procedure is just Claude instructions. Skill says 'call Workflow tool with script X' → Claude does it. Skills become workflow EMITTERS: gather context → generate JS script → call Workflow tool → report results.
4. Overlap: both handle multi-step + parallel agent work. Gap: Workflow has deterministic JS control flow, resume/journal, structured output schema, pipeline/parallel primitives, concurrency cap. Brana advantage: skills have session context, MCP tools, user prefs. Workflow advantage: true parallelism, resume after failure, progress monitoring.
Key insight: skills should coordinate context; workflows handle compute-intensive fan-out phases. Hybrid architecture is the goal.
tasks.description: Explore CC native /workflows command and dynamic workflow capabilities.
tasks.subject: Research /workflows command and dynamic workflows in Claude Code

tasks.context:
FINDINGS (2026-06-03):
Architecture options: A) Agent tool inline (current — sequential), B) Workflow tool (deterministic scripts, true parallelism), C) Hybrid (skills gather context/coordinate, workflows fan out).
Which skills benefit most from agent teams:
- /brana:research — multi-source web search parallelizes naturally (already exists as deep-research skill with Workflow)
- /brana:challenge — multiple challenger agents with different lenses (correctness/security/perf) per finding
- /brana:build SPECIFY step — [researcher, spec-writer, risk-scout] in parallel
- /brana:close — parallel: debrief + session-write + doc-check + memory-prune
Patterns to use: pipeline() for sequential-with-fan-out, parallel() for true barriers (all-needed-before-next), adversarial verify (N skeptics per finding).
Cost/latency: true parallel = Nx faster but Nx token cost. For tight loops (fix, log, do) overhead not worth it. For research/challenge/build: high ROI.
Recommended arch: skills remain coordinator layer (gates, decisions, context). Workflow scripts handle compute phases. Skills emit workflows, don't inline-compute them.
Next step: pilot with /brana:challenge (spawn 3 lens-agents per finding via Workflow). Task: t-1829 (if not exists, create).
tasks.description: Explore architecture where brana skills define process/steps but each step spawns a team of specialized agents in parallel.
tasks.subject: Research: skills as orchestrators of agent teams

tasks.context:
Four-wave compounding stack. Low output early, exponential after session ~30.

Wave 1 — Auto-Learning Engine: t-1713, t-1572, t-1668, t-653
Wave 2 — Knowledge Throughput: t-1202, t-1781, t-1804, t-1794 (fix first)
Wave 3 — Harness Hardening: t-1768, t-1565, t-889
Wave 4 — Multi-Agent Unlock: t-1587, t-1583, t-1594

Start: Wave 1 + Wave 3 in parallel (no blockers, S-M effort). Wave 2 follows. Wave 4 after harness is solid.

Compounding logic:
- Better memory recall → richer context per session → higher-quality builds → faster learning → repeat
- Sessions 1-10: foundation laying
- Sessions 10-30: compounding
- Sessions 30+: exponential (3-5x output)

tasks.context:
Implement the architecture from docs/ideas/memory-consolidation-autodream.md (challenger-reviewed 2026-06-03). This is brana's own Kairos/autoDream equivalent — Auto Dream is feature-flagged off in CC and not shipping soon.

AC: system/scripts/memory-consolidation.sh exists
AC: file ~/.swarm/lint-heal-state.json contains "last_consolidation_ts"
AC: memory-consolidation job entry exists in system/scheduler/scheduler.template.json
AC: system/scripts/memory-consolidation.sh contains "debrief-flags.jsonl"
AC: "cargo test" passes OR shell tests pass for threshold OR logic

6 implementation steps (from spec doc):
1. Extend lint-heal-state.json schema — add last_consolidation_ts: 0 field
2. Add to /brana:close: (a) increment session_count_since_run; (b) regex-scan errata for memory filenames; (c) append to ~/.swarm/debrief-flags.jsonl
3. Write system/scripts/memory-consolidation.sh — threshold check (OR logic: >24h OR >=5 sessions) + debrief-flag consumption + date normalization (frontmatter only) + consolidation log + update last_consolidation_ts
4. Register memory-consolidation in scheduler.template.json (Mon-Sat 15:30, enabled: true)
5. Write ADR — trigger model, state file extension, flag schema, scope split vs lint-heal
6. Write tests — threshold OR logic, flag-consumption idempotency, regex false-positive guard, date normalization edge cases

Key constraints (all from challenger review):
- Does NOT call lint-heal.sh (avoids lock collision)
- Date normalization on frontmatter created:/updated: fields ONLY — never body prose
- OR trigger logic (not AND) — ensures 24h fallback covers drift when close is skipped
- Atomic state file writes (tmp + mv)
- Debrief-flag consumption is idempotent (checks file existence before archiving)
tasks.notes: Fully implemented 2026-06-03. Pipeline end-to-end: close.md writes debrief flags + increments session counter → memory-consolidation.sh consumes flags + triggers on OR logic. 7 tests green. ADR-048 written. Merged to main.
tasks.subject: Implement memory-consolidation.sh — threshold-triggered memory consolidation with debrief integration

Max 2 iterations hard cap prevents P7 Recursive Decomposition
Spec_says field in findings output is mandatory — gives repair BUILD a precise target
2026-06-08: brainstorm challenge confirmed input contract and iteration cap are the two load-bearing implementation details
tasks.description: Add '### Challenger Gate' section between docs generation and CLOSE in system/procedures/build.md. Applies to all strategies except spike/investigation. Invocation rules: M+ mandatory (no prompt), S + system/hooks/decisions mandatory (no prompt), S regular paths = prompt with default 'Run Challenger'. Input contract: task spec text + git diff HEAD + task context/AC only. Blocking: score >= 4 → CLOSE blocked. Repair loop: structured findings {severity, ac_violated, description, file, spec_says}, user choice [Fix now | Override + reason | Abandon], max 2 iterations, then unconditional user surface. Findings appended to task context as Reflexion sr_t.
tasks.notes: Challenger Gate section inserted in build.md. All AC green. Feature/BugFix/Refactor/Greenfield/Migration all wired.
